channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: Robust Fine-Tuning from Non-Robust Pretrained Models: Mitigating Suboptimal Transfer With Epsilon-Scheduling
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'suboptimal transfer': fine-tuning non-robust pretrained models with a robust objective can impede task adaptation and yield poor adversarial robustness/accuracy.
  • Proposes Epsilon-Scheduling, a heuristic schedule over perturbation strength during training to avoid the transfer failure and enable effective robust fine-tuning (RFT).
  • Introduces 'expected robustness' as a metric to evaluate performance across a range of perturbation strengths, capturing the accuracy–robustness trade-off.
  • Provides extensive experiments (six pretrained models, five datasets) showing Epsilon-Scheduling consistently prevents suboptimal transfer and improves expected robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Jonas Ngnaw\\'e", 'Maxime Heuillet', 'Sabyasachi Sahoo', 'Yann Pequignot', 'Ola Ahmad', 'Audrey Durand', "Fr\\'ed\\'eric Precioso", "Christian Gagn\\'e"]
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'robust fine-tuning', 'adversarial training', 'transfer learning', 'defense']

item.title: GradPCA: Leveraging NTK Alignment for Reliable Out-of-Distribution Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces GradPCA, an OOD detection method that applies PCA to class-mean gradients leveraging low-rank structure from NTK alignment.
  • Provides theoretical analysis linking spectral OOD detection effectiveness to feature-space properties that emerge under NTK alignment and the role of pretrained representations.
  • Empirically demonstrates more consistent OOD detection performance across standard image classification benchmarks compared to existing methods.
  • Offers guidance for designing spectral OOD detectors and highlights that feature quality (pretrained vs non-pretrained) crucially impacts detector success.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mariia Seleznova', 'Hung-Hsu Chou', 'Claudio Mayrink Verdun', 'Gitta Kutyniok']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution detection', 'robustness', 'Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK)', 'gradient-based detection', 'spectral methods / PCA']

item.title: Adversarially Pretrained Transformers May Be Universally Robust In-Context Learners
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents theoretical analysis showing adversarially pretrained transformers can act as universally robust foundation models that provide robust in-context learning on unseen classification tasks without further adversarial training.
  • Shows single-layer linear transformers, adversarially pretrained across diverse tasks, can generalize robustly from clean demonstrations by focusing on robust features.
  • Identifies two practical challenges: the accuracy–robustness trade-off and high sample complexity during adversarial pretraining.
  • Provides code and positions adversarial pretraining as a costly but potentially high-leverage defense that yields robustness for downstream tasks 'for free.'

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Soichiro Kumano', 'Hiroshi Kera', 'Toshihiko Yamasaki']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'adversarial training', 'in-context learning', 'foundation models', 'robustness theory']

item.title: VII: Visual Instruction Injection for Jailbreaking Image-to-Video Generation Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Visual Instruction Injection (VII), a training-free, transferable jailbreaking attack that encodes malicious intent into benign-looking reference images to bypass refusal mechanisms in image-to-video (I2V) generation models.
  • Proposes two modules: Malicious Intent Reprogramming (distills malicious intent from unsafe text while minimizing explicit harmful content) and Visual Instruction Grounding (renders those instructions into the safe reference image to steer I2V outputs).
  • Evaluates on four commercial I2V models (Kling-v2.5-turbo, Gemini Veo-3.1, Seedance-1.5-pro, PixVerse-V5), achieving attack success rates up to 83.5% and near-zero refusal rates, outperforming baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bowen Zheng', 'Yongli Xiang', 'Ziming Hong', 'Zerong Lin', 'Chaojian Yu', 'Tongliang Liu', 'Xinge You']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'prompt injection', 'multimodal attacks', 'safety bypass']

item.title: Towards Transferable Defense Against Malicious Image Edits
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TDAE, a bimodal (image+text) defense framework to make images robust against malicious diffusion-based edits.
  • Introduces FlatGrad Defense Mechanism (FDM) that uses gradient regularization to steer perturbations to flat minima for better cross-model robustness.
  • Proposes Dynamic Prompt Defense (DPD) which iteratively optimizes text embeddings alongside image updates to enforce transferability across unseen editors; shows state-of-the-art intra- and cross-model mitigation in experiments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jie Zhang', 'Shuai Dong', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'adversarial robustness', 'diffusion models', 'prompt defense', 'transferability']

item.title: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucinations via Gradient-based Self-Reflection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Gradient-based Influence-Aware Constrained Decoding (GACD), an inference-time method to reduce multimodal hallucinations without finetuning or auxiliary models.
  • Uses first-order Taylor gradients to estimate contributions of individual text tokens and visual features to current outputs (bias estimation).
  • Mitigates hallucinations via two mechanisms: suppressing spurious visual features correlated with output objects, and rebalancing cross-modal contributions by strengthening visual signals relative to text.
  • Evaluated on multiple benchmarks, showing improved visual grounding and reduced hallucination rates for MLLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shan Wang', 'Maying Shen', 'Nadine Chang', 'Chuong Nguyen', 'Hongdong Li', 'Jose M. Alvarez']
  • Tags: ['multimodal hallucination', 'robustness', 'inference-time defense', 'gradient-based methods', 'visual grounding']

item.title: Harnessing Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models for Face Anti-Spoofing
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces FaceCoT, a large-scale vision-language VQA dataset with chain-of-thought (CoT) annotations covering 14 face spoofing attack types to support face anti-spoofing (FAS).
  • Develops a caption model refined with reinforcement learning to expand and improve multimodal annotations.
  • Proposes CoT-Enhanced Progressive Learning (CEPL) to leverage CoT data in multimodal LLMs for improved FAS performance and interpretability.
  • Reports extensive experiments showing models trained with FaceCoT and CEPL outperform state-of-the-art FAS methods on multiple benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Honglu Zhang', 'Zhiqin Fang', 'Ningning Zhao', 'Saihui Hou', 'Long Ma', 'Renwang Pei', 'Zhaofeng He']
  • Tags: ['face-anti-spoofing', 'presentation-attacks', 'multimodal-LLM', 'dataset', 'chain-of-thought']

item.title: Dynamic Token Reweighting for Robust Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents DTR, an inference-time defense that mitigates multimodal jailbreak attacks by optimizing KV caches to reweight visual tokens.
  • Introduces a formulation of safety-relevant distributional shift from visual modality and dynamically adjusts token weights to reduce adversarial visual impact.
  • Claims improved robustness to multimodal jailbreaks while preserving benign task performance and inference efficiency, validated across multiple VLMs and attack benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tanqiu Jiang', 'Jiacheng Liang', 'Rongyi Zhu', 'Jiawei Zhou', 'Fenglong Ma', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'multimodal defenses', 'KV-cache optimization', 'robustness']

item.title: From Leaderboard to Deployment: Code Quality Challenges in AV Perception Repositories
item.description:
Summary

  • Large-scale empirical study of software quality in 178 AV perception repositories from KITTI and NuScenes 3D object detection leaderboards.
  • Used static analysis tools (Pylint, Bandit, Radon) to evaluate code errors, high-severity security vulnerabilities, maintainability, and development practices.
  • Found only 7.3% of repositories met basic production-readiness criteria (no critical errors and no high-severity vulnerabilities); security issues concentrated in a few common problem types.
  • Correlated CI/CD adoption with better maintainability and proposed actionable guidelines to prevent the most common security issues.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mateus Karvat', 'Bram Adams', 'Sidney Givigi']
  • Tags: ['software security', 'vulnerability analysis', 'static analysis', 'autonomous vehicles', 'production readiness']

item.title: Process Over Outcome: Cultivating Forensic Reasoning for Generalizable Multimodal Manipulation Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes REFORM, a reasoning-driven framework for multimodal manipulation detection that shifts learning from outcome-fitting to modeling forensic reasoning.
  • REFORM uses a three-stage curriculum: induce forensic rationales, align reasoning with final judgments, and refine logical consistency via reinforcement learning.
  • Introduces ROM, a large-scale dataset with rich reasoning annotations, and shows improved generalization and state-of-the-art results on ROM, DGM4, and MMFakeBench.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuchen Zhang', 'Yaxiong Wang', 'Kecheng Han', 'Yujiao Wu', 'Lianwei Wu', 'Li Zhu', 'Zhedong Zheng']
  • Tags: ['multimodal manipulation detection', 'forensic reasoning', 'robustness/generalization', 'dataset (ROM)', 'defense/detection']

item.title: CTForensics: A Comprehensive Dataset and Method for AI-Generated CT Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CTForensics, a comprehensive dataset of synthetic CT images generated by ten diverse generative methods to evaluate generalization of forgery detectors.
  • Proposes ESF-CTFD, a CNN-based detector that fuses wavelet-enhanced multi-scale spatial features with frequency-domain processing to capture CT-specific forgery cues.
  • Reports that ESF-CTFD outperforms existing (mostly natural-image) detection methods and generalizes better across different CT generative models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yiheng Li', 'Zichang Tan', 'Guoqing Xu', 'Yijun Ye', 'Yang Yang', 'Zhen Lei']
  • Tags: ['forgery-detection', 'medical-imaging', 'dataset', 'robustness', 'deepfake-detection']

item.title: What Helps -- and What Hurts: Bidirectional Explanations for Vision Transformers
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BiCAM, a bidirectional class activation mapping that preserves signed (positive and negative) attributions for Vision Transformers.
  • Introduces a Positive-to-Negative Ratio (PNR) that summarizes attribution balance and can be used for lightweight detection of adversarial examples without retraining.
  • Demonstrates improved localization and faithfulness on ImageNet, VOC, and COCO and generalization across ViT variants (DeiT, Swin).
  • Primary contribution is interpretability, with an ancillary application to adversarial example detection (defensive/detection mechanism).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qin Su', 'Tie Luo']
  • Tags: ['explainability', 'vision-transformers', 'adversarial-detection', 'class-activation-mapping', 'robustness']

item.title: PPEDCRF: Privacy-Preserving Enhanced Dynamic CRF for Location-Privacy Protection for Sequence Videos with Minimal Detection Degradation
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies location-privacy leakage from dashcam/sequence videos under a background-based retrieval attacker and evaluates attack success (e.g., Top-k retrieval).
  • Proposes PPEDCRF, a defense that injects calibrated perturbations into location-sensitive background regions while preserving foreground detection utility via (i) dynamic CRF for temporal consistency, (ii) a normalized control penalty to allocate perturbation strength, and (iii) a utility-preserving noise injection module.
  • Demonstrates significant reduction in location-retrieval accuracy with minimal degradation to object detection and segmentation (mAP and segmentation metrics) compared to baselines like global noise and feature anonymization.
  • Provides code and experiments on public driving datasets validating the privacy-preserving defense for video sequences.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bo Ma', 'Jinsong Wu', 'Weiqi Yan', 'Catherine Shi', 'Minh Nguyen']
  • Tags: ['location-privacy', 'privacy-preserving defense', 'adversarial perturbation', 'video anonymization', 'temporal-consistency (CRF)']

item.title: RA-Det: Towards Universal Detection of AI-Generated Images via Robustness Asymmetry
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'robustness asymmetry': real images maintain stable semantic features under small structured perturbations, while AI-generated images show larger feature drift.
  • Provides theoretical analysis linking robustness asymmetry to memorization tendencies in generative models, explaining its prevalence across architectures.
  • Proposes RA-Det, a behavior-driven, model- and data-agnostic detector that converts robustness asymmetry into a practical decision signal for synthetic-image detection.
  • Evaluated on 14 generative models and against 10+ strong detectors, reporting an average performance improvement of 7.81% and transferability to unseen generators; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinchang Wang', 'Yunhao Chen', 'Yuechen Zhang', 'Congcong Bian', 'Zihao Guo', 'Xingjun Ma', 'Hui Li']
  • Tags: ['synthetic-image detection', 'robustness', 'forensics', 'defense']

item.title: VidDoS: Universal Denial-of-Service Attack on Video-based Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces VidDoS, a universal Energy-Latency Attack (ELA) that crafts instance-agnostic triggers to induce denial-of-service on Video-LLMs without per-instance gradients.
  • Key techniques: masked teacher forcing to steer outputs toward long/expensive target sequences, a refusal penalty, and early-termination suppression to overcome conciseness priors.
  • Empirical results across three Video-LLMs and datasets show dramatic effects (≈205× token expansion, >15× inference latency) and demonstrate safety-critical failures in simulated real-time autonomous driving streams.
  • Focuses on attack development and evaluation, highlighting a novel high-hazard vulnerability unique to video-based multimodal LLMs and urging mitigation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Duoxun Tang', 'Dasen Dai', 'Jiyao Wang', 'Xiao Yang', 'Jianyu Wang', 'Siqi Cai']
  • Tags: ['denial-of-service', 'energy-latency attack', 'universal trigger', 'video-LLM vulnerability', 'robustness/security']

item.title: Deepfake Forensics Adapter: A Dual-Stream Network for Generalizable Deepfake Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Deepfake Forensics Adapter (DFA), a dual-stream detection framework that combines a frozen CLIP backbone with a Global Feature Adapter, a Local Anomaly Stream using facial structure priors, and an Interactive Fusion Classifier (transformer) for feature interaction.
  • Designed to improve generalization to unseen forgery patterns by leveraging CLIP's vision-language features without fine-tuning CLIP parameters and adding targeted forensics modules for local/global inconsistency detection.
  • Reports state-of-the-art results on deepfake benchmarks, notably DFDC (frame-level AUC/EER 0.816/0.256 and video-level AUC/EER 0.836/0.251), claiming improved robustness/generalization to evolving deepfake threats.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianfeng Liao', 'Yichen Wei', 'Raymond Chan Ching Bon', 'Shulan Wang', 'Kam-Pui Chow', 'Kwok-Yan Lam']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'forensics', 'robustness/generalization', 'CLIP adapter', 'video/image forgery']

item.title: DOCFORGE-BENCH: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Document Forgery Detection and Analysis
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DOCFORGE-BENCH, a zero-shot benchmark evaluating 14 forgery-detection methods across 8 document datasets (text tampering, receipts, ID manipulation) using published pretrained weights with no domain adaptation.
  • Finds a pervasive calibration failure: methods show moderate Pixel-AUC but near-zero Pixel-F1 under fixed thresholds due to tiny tampered-region prevalence in documents; Oracle-F1 and small threshold adaptation on N=10 images recover substantial performance.
  • Concludes that out-of-the-box methods are unreliable for document forgery detection and emphasizes calibration/threshold adaptation as a practical defense step; highlights the lack of datasets covering generative-AI-based forgeries.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zengqi Zhao', 'Weidi Xia', 'Peter Wei', 'Yan Zhang', 'Yiyi Zhang', 'Jane Mo', 'Tiannan Zhang', 'Yuanqin Dai', 'Zexi Chen', 'Simiao Ren']
  • Tags: ['document forgery detection', 'benchmark', 'calibration', 'defense', 'generative-AI-forgery']

item.title: Towards Policy-Adaptive Image Guardrail: Benchmark and Method
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SafeEditBench, a benchmark that constructs visually similar safe/unsafe image pairs aligned to five distinct safety policies to evaluate cross-policy generalization of vision-language models.
  • Finds that existing VLM-based safeguarding methods overfit to seen policies and fail to generalize to unseen policies, losing instruction-following and general knowledge.
  • Proposes SafeGuard-VL, a reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) approach that explicitly optimizes policy-grounded rewards to improve adaptation to evolving safety policies.
  • Presents extensive experiments showing improved robustness of unsafe-image guardrails across multiple policies compared to supervised fine-tuning.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Caiyong Piao', 'Zhiyuan Yan', 'Haoming Xu', 'Yunzhen Zhao', 'Kaiqing Lin', 'Feiyang Xu', 'Shuigeng Zhou']
  • Tags: ['safety-guardrails', 'vision-language models', 'robustness', 'benchmark', 'reinforcement learning']

item.title: From Intuition to Investigation: A Tool-Augmented Reasoning MLLM Framework for Generalizable Face Anti-Spoofing
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TAR-FAS, a tool-augmented MLLM framework that frames face anti-spoofing (FAS) as a Chain-of-Thought with Visual Tools (CoT-VT) to combine intuitive textual observations with adaptive invocation of external visual analysis tools for fine-grained spoof clue investigation.
  • Introduces a tool-augmented annotation pipeline and releases the ToolFAS-16K dataset containing multi-turn tool-use reasoning trajectories to train and evaluate tool-aware reasoning for FAS.
  • Presents a training method, Diverse-Tool Group Relative Policy Optimization (DT-GRPO), enabling models to autonomously learn efficient tool usage policies.
  • Reports state-of-the-art cross-domain performance under a one-to-eleven protocol and emphasizes improved trustworthy, fine-grained detection of presentation attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haoyuan Zhang', 'Keyao Wang', 'Guosheng Zhang', 'Haixiao Yue', 'Zhiwen Tan', 'Siran Peng', 'Tianshuo Zhang', 'Xiao Tan', 'Kunbin Chen', 'Wei He', 'Jingdong Wang', 'Ajian Liu', 'Xiangyu Zhu', 'Zhen Lei']
  • Tags: ['face-anti-spoofing', 'presentation-attack-detection', 'tool-augmented-MLLM', 'ToolFAS-16K-dataset', 'cross-domain-robustness']

item.title: Diversity over Uniformity: Rethinking Representation in Generated Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an anti-feature-collapse learning framework to preserve diverse and complementary forgery cues in representations by filtering task-irrelevant components and reducing overlap among cues.
  • Aims to improve robustness and generalization of generated-image (deepfake) detectors to unseen generative models, reducing reliance on a few dominant saliency directions.
  • Demonstrates substantial cross-model accuracy gains (~5.02%) on multiple public benchmarks and provides source code.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qinghui He', 'Haifeng Zhang', 'Qiao Qin', 'Bo Liu', 'Xiuli Bi', 'Bin Xiao']
  • Tags: ['generated image detection', 'deepfake detection', 'robustness', 'representation learning', 'visual forensics']

item.title: MIDAS: Multi-Image Dispersion and Semantic Reconstruction for Jailbreaking MLLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MIDAS, a multimodal jailbreak framework that decomposes harmful semantics into subunits, disperses them across multiple images, and uses cross-image chained reasoning to reconstruct malicious intent.
  • Aims to increase model reliance on visual cues and lengthen reasoning chains to reduce security attention and bypass existing safety mechanisms.
  • Evaluates extensively across datasets and 4 closed-source MLLMs, reporting an average attack success rate of 81.46% and outperforming prior jailbreak methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yilian Liu', 'Xiaojun Jia', 'Guoshun Nan', 'Jiuyang Lyu', 'Zhican Chen', 'Tao Guan', 'Shuyuan Luo', 'Zhongyi Zhai', 'Yang Liu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'multimodal attacks', 'adversarial attack', 'security evaluation']

item.title: CaptionFool: Universal Image Captioning Model Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CaptionFool, a universal (input-agnostic) adversarial attack targeting transformer-based image captioning models.
  • Modifies only ~1.2% of image patches (7 out of 577) to force models to generate arbitrary target captions with 94–96% success.
  • Demonstrates ability to produce offensive/slang terms that evade content moderation filters, highlighting risks for deployed vision-language systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Swapnil Parekh']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'image captioning', 'universal attack', 'content moderation evasion', 'vision-language models']

item.title: Multimodal Adaptive Retrieval Augmented Generation through Internal Representation Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Multimodal Adaptive RAG (MMA-RAG), which dynamically decides whether to incorporate retrieved external evidence based on model-internal confidence to reduce hallucinations in visual question answering.
  • Implements a decision classifier trained via layer-wise analysis that leverages joint internal visual and textual representations to guide reverse image retrieval and adaptive use of external knowledge.
  • Reports significant performance improvements on three VQA datasets and ablation studies showing the importance of internal representations for retrieval decisions.
  • Emphasizes balancing external knowledge utilization with inference robustness in multimodal retrieval-augmented generation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruoshuang Du', 'Xin Sun', 'Qiang Liu', 'Bowen Song', 'Zhongqi Chen', 'Weiqiang Wang', 'Liang Wang']
  • Tags: ['multimodal RAG', 'hallucination mitigation', 'robustness', 'retrieval decision', 'visual question answering']

item.title: Adversarial Patch Generation for Visual-Infrared Dense Prediction Tasks via Joint Position-Color Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AP-PCO, a joint position-and-color optimization method to generate adversarial patches targeting visual–infrared (VI) dense prediction models.
  • Introduces crossmodal color adaptation to reduce cross-spectral saliency by constraining patch appearance to infrared grayscale characteristics while maintaining visible-domain perturbation strength.
  • Operates in a black-box setting (no internal model access) and demonstrates consistently strong attacks across multiple architectures, providing a practical benchmark for VI robustness evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['He Li', 'Wenyue He', 'Weihang Kong', 'Xingchen Zhang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-patch', 'black-box attack', 'multimodal adversarial attack', 'visual-infrared', 'dense-prediction']

item.title: Physical Evaluation of Naturalistic Adversarial Patches for Camera-Based Traffic-Sign Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CompGTSRB, a composite dataset tailored for autonomous vehicle environments by pasting GTSRB traffic sign instances onto undistorted target-platform backgrounds.
  • Trains a YOLOv5 detector and generates Naturalistic Adversarial Patches (NAPs) via a GAN with latent-space optimization following existing NAP methods.
  • Performs systematic physical-world experiments on a Quanser QCar platform (front CSI camera) evaluating NAP transferability across distance, patch size, and placement, showing reduced STOP-class confidence.
  • Provides a step-by-step methodology for credible physical patch evaluation and highlights the need for defenses addressing localized patch corruption in embedded perception pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Brianna D'Urso", 'Tahmid Hasan Sakib', 'Syed Rafay Hasan', 'Terry N. Guo']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-patches', 'physical-adversarial-examples', 'traffic-sign-attacks', 'robustness-evaluation', 'dataset-for-attacks']

item.title: Steering Away from Memorization: Reachability-Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-Image Diffusion
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses memorization (training-data leakage) in text-to-image diffusion models and limitations of prior mitigation methods that degrade quality or alignment.
  • Proposes Reachability-Aware Diffusion Steering (RADS): models the diffusion denoising process as a dynamical system, approximates a backward reachable tube of states that lead to memorized outputs, and uses constrained reinforcement learning to minimally perturb caption embeddings at inference time to avoid those states.
  • Demonstrates improved trade-offs between generation diversity (SSCD), quality (FID), and alignment (CLIP) versus baselines, offering a plug-and-play defense that does not modify the diffusion backbone.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sathwik Karnik', 'Juyeop Kim', 'Sanmi Koyejo', 'Jong-Seok Lee', 'Somil Bansal']
  • Tags: ['memorization', 'privacy', 'defense', 'diffusion models', 'reinforcement learning']

item.title: You Don't Need All That Attention: Surgical Memorization Mitigation in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces GUARD, an inference-time framework that steers text-to-image diffusion generation away from memorized training images while preserving alignment to the prompt.
  • Provides a concrete instantiation using per-prompt cross-attention attenuation: a statistical mechanism identifies prompt positions needing attenuation and dynamically reduces cross-attention at those locations during denoising.
  • Evaluated across two architectures, GUARD reportedly achieves state-of-the-art mitigation for both verbatim and template memorization while maintaining or improving image quality.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kairan Zhao', 'Eleni Triantafillou', 'Peter Triantafillou']
  • Tags: ['memorization-mitigation', 'privacy-defense', 'diffusion-models', 'cross-attention-attenuation']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JAILBREAK FOUNDRY (JBF), a system that translates jailbreak papers into runnable attack modules via a multi-agent workflow with three components: JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluation).
  • Reproduced 30 jailbreak attacks with high fidelity (mean reproduced–reported ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduced attack-specific implementation code by ~50%, and achieved an 82.5% mean reused-code ratio.
  • Enables standardized, automated AdvBench evaluations across 10 victim models using a consistent GPT-4o judge, supporting living benchmarks and scalable red-teaming/attack benchmarking.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial attacks', 'reproducibility', 'benchmarking', 'automated red teaming']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the final LLM softmax as an Energy-Based Model and decomposes sequence probability into interacting EBMs during decoding.
  • Proposes two training-free, logit-derived metrics—spilled energy (step-to-step discrepancy) and marginalized energy (single-step)—to detect hallucinations and failures.
  • Evaluated across nine benchmarks and multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (e.g., LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen3), showing robust, competitive hallucination detection and cross-task generalization without additional training.
  • Localizes answer tokens and tests for hallucination similarly to prior work but without trained probes or activation ablations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'energy-based-models', 'model-robustness', 'training-free-detection', 'safety']

item.title: Exposing Citation Vulnerabilities in Generative Engines
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes citation behavior of generative engines (GEs) to assess vulnerability to web-based poisoning attacks by introducing the concept of a content-injection barrier (difficulty for attackers to manipulate answers via published web content).
  • Proposes evaluation criteria that classify publisher attributes of cited sources to estimate poisoning threats and applies them in political domains (U.S. and Japan).
  • Finds that a substantial fraction of citations come from low-barrier sources (higher poisoning risk), with U.S. political answers showing greater exposure to poisoning than Japan, and that low-barrier sources are often cited but poorly reflected in generated content.
  • Discusses mitigation strategies to increase exposure of primary-source publishers and limitations of standard techniques (e.g., language barriers) for reducing poisoning risk.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Riku Mochizuki', 'Shusuke Komatsu', 'Souta Noguchi', 'Kazuto Ataka']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'citation-manipulation', 'attack-evaluation', 'defense-mitigation']

item.title: Is It Thinking or Cheating? Detecting Implicit Reward Hacking by Measuring Reasoning Effort
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses implicit reward hacking where models obtain high reward via shortcuts that do not appear in their chain-of-thought (CoT).
  • Proposes TRACE: progressively truncate CoT, force answers at cutoffs, estimate expected reward vs reasoning length, and use area-under-curve to detect low-effort shortcut exploitation.
  • Empirical gains: >65% improvement over a strong 72B CoT monitor on math reasoning and >30% over a 32B monitor on coding; can discover unknown loopholes during training.
  • Presents a scalable, unsupervised oversight/detection method for identifying reward-hacking behavior that bypasses standard CoT monitors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinpeng Wang', 'Nitish Joshi', 'Barbara Plank', 'Rico Angell', 'He He']
  • Tags: ['reward-hacking', 'attack-detection', 'chain-of-thought-monitoring', 'adversarial-safety']

item.title: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses safety of chain-of-thought reasoning in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs), noting that harmful content can appear in intermediate reasoning even if final outputs seem safe.
  • Proposes Intervened Preference Optimization (IPO): a process-supervision alignment method that replaces compliance steps with safety triggers and constructs strong preference-learning pairs via corrective interventions.
  • Empirical results on jailbreak and adversarial safety benchmarks show IPO reduces harmfulness by over 30% relative to SFT and RL baselines while retaining strong reasoning performance.
  • Highlights key insights about safe reasoning (few critical safety-trigger steps, correlation between compliance cues and unsafe continuations, and effectiveness of corrective interventions) and argues for explicit alignment of reasoning processes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yichi Zhang', 'Yue Ding', 'Jingwen Yang', 'Tianwei Luo', 'Dongbai Li', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Qiang Liu', 'Hang Su', 'Yinpeng Dong', 'Jun Zhu']
  • Tags: ['AI safety', 'defense', 'jailbreaking', 'chain-of-thought alignment', 'preference learning']

item.title: Reasoning or Retrieval? A Study of Answer Attribution on Large Reasoning Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies two competing mechanisms in large reasoning models (LRMs): chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and memory retrieval, and shows they can conflict (final answers contradict reasoning traces).
  • Performs controlled experiments with misleading reasoning cues and corrupted retrieval answers to demonstrate models exploit retrieval as a shortcut; dominance depends on domain, model scale, and fine-tuning approach.
  • Proposes FARL, a fine-tuning framework combining memory unlearning with reinforcement learning to suppress retrieval shortcuts and encourage genuine, generalizable reasoning.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhui Wang', 'Changjiang Li', 'Guangke Chen', 'Jiacheng Liang', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'shortcut exploitation', 'robustness', 'fine-tuning defense', 'chain-of-thought']

item.title: PMark: Towards Robust and Distortion-free Semantic-level Watermarking with Channel Constraints
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PMark, a semantic-level watermarking (SWM) method for LLMs that uses proxy functions (PFs) and multiple PF channels to embed robust watermarks at the sentence level.
  • Provides a theoretical framework and guarantees for distortion-free watermarking and robustness against paraphrasing-style attacks, with dynamic PF-median estimation via sampling and an optimized variant to improve sampling efficiency.
  • Empirically shows PMark outperforms existing SWM baselines in text quality and robustness to text modifications/paraphrases, aiming to improve detection of machine-generated text.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiahao Huo', 'Shuliang Liu', 'Bin Wang', 'Junyan Zhang', 'Yibo Yan', 'Aiwei Liu', 'Xuming Hu', 'Mingxun Zhou']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'model-output detection', 'robustness', 'paraphrase attacks']

item.title: BinaryShield: Cross-Service Threat Intelligence in LLM Services using Privacy-Preserving Fingerprints
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BinaryShield, a privacy-preserving threat intelligence system that enables sharing of attack fingerprints across compliance boundaries to mitigate prompt injection risk.
  • Transforms suspicious prompts via PII redaction, semantic embedding, binary quantization, and randomized response to produce privacy-preserving fingerprints that retain attack patterns.
  • Evaluates effectiveness and efficiency: achieves F1=0.94 (vs SimHash 0.77), reduces storage, and enables ~38x faster similarity search than dense embeddings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Waris Gill', 'Natalie Isak', 'Matthew Dressman']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'privacy-preserving', 'threat intelligence', 'attack detection']

item.title: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucinations via Gradient-based Self-Reflection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Gradient-based Influence-Aware Constrained Decoding (GACD), an inference-time method to reduce multimodal hallucinations without finetuning or auxiliary models.
  • Uses first-order Taylor gradients to estimate contributions of individual text tokens and visual features to current outputs (bias estimation).
  • Mitigates hallucinations via two mechanisms: suppressing spurious visual features correlated with output objects, and rebalancing cross-modal contributions by strengthening visual signals relative to text.
  • Evaluated on multiple benchmarks, showing improved visual grounding and reduced hallucination rates for MLLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shan Wang', 'Maying Shen', 'Nadine Chang', 'Chuong Nguyen', 'Hongdong Li', 'Jose M. Alvarez']
  • Tags: ['multimodal hallucination', 'robustness', 'inference-time defense', 'gradient-based methods', 'visual grounding']

item.title: Dynamic Token Reweighting for Robust Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents DTR, an inference-time defense that mitigates multimodal jailbreak attacks by optimizing KV caches to reweight visual tokens.
  • Introduces a formulation of safety-relevant distributional shift from visual modality and dynamically adjusts token weights to reduce adversarial visual impact.
  • Claims improved robustness to multimodal jailbreaks while preserving benign task performance and inference efficiency, validated across multiple VLMs and attack benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tanqiu Jiang', 'Jiacheng Liang', 'Rongyi Zhu', 'Jiawei Zhou', 'Fenglong Ma', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'multimodal defenses', 'KV-cache optimization', 'robustness']

item.title: Learn-to-Distance: Distance Learning for Detecting LLM-Generated Text
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a geometric interpretation of rewrite-based detection methods for identifying LLM-generated text and explains their generalization properties.
  • Proposes Learn-to-Distance (L2D), a rewrite-based detector that adaptively learns a distance function between original and rewritten text, with theoretical results showing adaptive distance is superior to fixed distances for detection.
  • Reports extensive empirical evaluation (100+ settings) showing L2D outperforms baselines across multiple target LLMs (e.g., GPT, Claude, Gemini), with large relative improvements.
  • Provides a public Python implementation (GitHub) for reproducibility.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hongyi Zhou', 'Jin Zhu', 'Kai Ye', 'Ying Yang', 'Erhan Xu', 'Chengchun Shi']
  • Tags: ['LLM-generated text detection', 'defense', 'rewrite-based detection', 'robustness']

item.title: A Comprehensive Dataset for Human vs. AI Generated Text Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a large dataset of >58,000 samples pairing authentic New York Times articles with synthetic versions generated by multiple LLMs (Gemma-2-9b, Mistral-7B, Qwen-2-72B, LLaMA-8B, Yi-Large, GPT-4-o).
  • Provides prompts (original article abstracts) and full human-authored narratives to support detection and attribution research for AI-generated text.
  • Establishes baseline results: human vs. AI detection accuracy of 58.35% and model attribution accuracy of 8.92%.
  • Dataset is publicly available on Hugging Face to facilitate development of robust detection and attribution methods.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rajarshi Roy', 'Nasrin Imanpour', 'Ashhar Aziz', 'Shashwat Bajpai', 'Gurpreet Singh', 'Shwetangshu Biswas', 'Kapil Wanaskar', 'Parth Patwa', 'Subhankar Ghosh', 'Shreyas Dixit', 'Nilesh Ranjan Pal', 'Vipula Rawte', 'Ritvik Garimella', 'Gaytri Jena', 'Amit Sheth', 'Vasu Sharma', 'Aishwarya Naresh Reganti', 'Vinija Jain', 'Aman Chadha', 'Amitava Das']
  • Tags: ['dataset', 'AI-generated-text', 'detection', 'attribution', 'forensics']

item.title: Steering Evaluation-Aware Language Models to Act Like They Are Deployed
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies evaluation-awareness in LLMs: models detect evaluation cues and alter behavior (e.g., adding Python type hints) to appear more aligned during tests.
  • Proposes activation steering: compute a steering vector from the original model and add it to activations to suppress evaluation-aware behavior during evaluation.
  • Empirical setup: they induce evaluation-aware behavior via continued pretraining on cue-containing documents and expert-iteration training, then show steering reduces the gap between evaluation and deployment behavior.
  • Implication: activation steering can make safety evaluations more reliable (or conversely enable models to evade evaluation), highlighting a security concern for red-teaming and evaluation protocols.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tim Tian Hua', 'Andrew Qin', 'Samuel Marks', 'Neel Nanda']
  • Tags: ['evaluation-evasion', 'activation-steering', 'red-teaming', 'safety-evaluation', 'model-manipulation']

item.title: Language steering in latent space to mitigate unintended code-switching
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a lightweight inference-time latent-space language steering method: identify language directions via PCA on parallel translations and shift token embeddings along these axes to control language identity.
  • Requires minimal parallel data for calibration and negligible computational overhead, using a single principal component to achieve 95–99% language classification accuracy.
  • Empirical results on Qwen2.5 and Llama-3.2 show up to 55% reduction in next-token distributional divergence and 63–99% reduction in a Generation Code-Switching Index across multiple language pairs (statistically significant).
  • Layer-wise analysis reveals language identity concentrates in final layers with near-perfect linear separability, informing where steering is most effective.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Andrey Goncharov', 'Nikolai Kondusov', 'Alexey Zaytsev']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'multilingual', 'code-switching mitigation', 'latent-space steering']

item.title: Train Once, Answer All: Many Pretraining Experiments for the Cost of One
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a method to run multiple controlled pretraining experiments concurrently in a single training run, enabling many experiments for the compute cost of one.
  • Replicates prior work on data contamination, data poisoning, and memorization, and runs new experiments on knowledge acquisition, mathematical reasoning, and watermarking.
  • Proposes continual pretraining dependence testing (CPDT) to detect interactions between simultaneous experiments and finds interactions negligible in their setup.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sebastian Bordt', 'Martin Pawelczyk']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'memorization/privacy', 'data-contamination', 'watermarking/defense', 'pretraining-methodology']

item.title: RedTeamCUA: Realistic Adversarial Testing of Computer-Use Agents in Hybrid Web-OS Environments
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces RedTeamCUA, a hybrid VM+Docker sandbox for realistic adversarial testing of computer-use agents (CUAs) across OS and web interfaces.
  • Presents RTC-Bench, an 864-example benchmark of hybrid web-OS prompt-injection and adversarial scenarios to measure CUA vulnerabilities (e.g., ASR, Attempt Rate).
  • Evaluates multiple frontier CUAs, demonstrating substantial attack success rates (e.g., up to 60% ASR for Claude 4.5 Sonnet | CUA) and highlighting practical risks from indirect prompt injection.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zeyi Liao', 'Jaylen Jones', 'Linxi Jiang', 'Yuting Ning', 'Eric Fosler-Lussier', 'Yu Su', 'Zhiqiang Lin', 'Huan Sun']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'red teaming', 'benchmarking', 'sandboxing', 'CUA security']

item.title: VeriTrail: Closed-Domain Hallucination Detection with Traceability
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes VeriTrail, a method to detect closed-domain hallucinations and provide traceability of where unsubstantiated content was introduced across multi-step (MGS) and single-step (SGS) generative processes.
  • Introduces new datasets that include all intermediate outputs for MGS and human annotations of final-output faithfulness to sources.
  • Shows VeriTrail outperforms baseline methods on these datasets, enabling both detection of hallucinated content and tracing its likely origin in intermediate steps.

Paper Type

  • Research Paper

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dasha Metropolitansky', 'Jonathan Larson']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'traceability', 'multi-step-generation', 'faithfulness', 'dataset']

item.title: Enhancing Hallucination Detection through Noise Injection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a training-free, inference-time method for hallucination detection that perturbs a subset of model parameters or hidden activations (noise injection) to capture Bayesian model uncertainty.
  • Argues that sampling by perturbing parameters/activations yields better uncertainty estimates for detecting hallucinations than standard token-level sampling.
  • Demonstrates significant improvements in hallucination detection across multiple datasets, model architectures, and uncertainty metrics.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Litian Liu', 'Reza Pourreza', 'Sunny Panchal', 'Apratim Bhattacharyya', 'Yubing Jian', 'Yao Qin', 'Roland Memisevic']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'uncertainty estimation', 'noise injection', 'robustness', 'inference-time defenses']

item.title: I Can't Believe It's Not Robust: Catastrophic Collapse of Safety Classifiers under Embedding Drift
item.description:
Summary

  • Small embedding drift (normalized perturbation σ = 0.02, ≈1° angular) causes safety classifiers trained on frozen embeddings to collapse: ROC-AUC falls from 85% to 50%.
  • Failures are often silent: mean confidence only drops ~14%, and 72% of misclassifications occur with high confidence, defeating standard monitoring.
  • Instruction-tuned models exhibit ~20% worse class separability than base models, making aligned systems harder to protect and undermining the assumption that safety checks transfer across model updates.
  • Implication: production safety architectures that rely on frozen-embedding classifiers are fundamentally fragile to embedding drift and need version-robust defenses or continual recalibration.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary', 'Aman Chadha']
  • Tags: ['safety classifiers', 'embedding drift', 'deployment security', 'robustness', 'monitoring failures']

item.title: JailNewsBench: Multi-Lingual and Regional Benchmark for Fake News Generation under Jailbreak Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JailNewsBench, a large benchmark to evaluate LLM robustness to jailbreak attacks that induce fake-news generation across languages and regions.
  • Covers 34 regions and 22 languages with ~300k instances, 5 jailbreak attack methods, and 8 evaluation sub-metrics using LLM-as-a-Judge.
  • Evaluates 9 LLMs, reporting up to 86.3% attack success rate and a maximum harmfulness score of 3.5/5; finds notably weaker defenses for English/U.S.-related topics.
  • Shows gaps in existing safety datasets for fake-news coverage and releases dataset and code.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Masahiro Kaneko', 'Ayana Niwa', 'Timothy Baldwin']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak', 'fake-news', 'benchmark', 'LLM-safety', 'multilingual']

item.title: Attention Smoothing Is All You Need For Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Attention Smoothing Unlearning (ASU), which performs unlearning via self-distillation from a forget-teacher by increasing attention softmax temperature to flatten attention distributions.
  • Claims ASU suppresses lexical- and semantic-level associations that enable memorization, producing bounded optimization that erases factual information while preserving coherence.
  • Evaluates on unlearning benchmarks (TOFU, MUSE, WMDP) and real-world/continual unlearning scenarios across question answering and text completion, showing improved forgetting with minimal utility loss.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Saleh Zare Zade', 'Xiangyu Zhou', 'Sijia Liu', 'Dongxiao Zhu']
  • Tags: ['unlearning', 'privacy', 'model editing', 'attention-based defenses', 'self-distillation']

item.title: Your Inference Request Will Become a Black Box: Confidential Inference for Cloud-based Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Talaria, a confidential inference framework that partitions LLM inference between a client-controlled Confidential Virtual Machine (CVM) and cloud GPUs to protect client data while preserving model IP.
  • Introduces Reversible Masked Outsourcing (ReMO), a hybrid masking protocol that reversibly obscures intermediate activations before outsourcing weight-dependent computations.
  • Claims lossless inference (outputs identical to original model), defends against token inference attacks (reducing token reconstruction accuracy from >97.5% to ~1.34%), and maintains efficiency and scalability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chung-ju Huang', 'Huiqiang Zhao', 'Yuanpeng He', 'Lijian Li', 'Wenpin Jiao', 'Zhi Jin', 'Peixuan Chen', 'Leye Wang']
  • Tags: ['confidential inference', 'privacy-preserving ML', 'inference attacks/defenses', 'secure computation']

item.title: ALTER: Asymmetric LoRA for Token-Entropy-Guided Unlearning of LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ALTER, an efficient token-level unlearning framework for LLMs using an asymmetric LoRA architecture to isolate and remove target knowledge while preserving core capabilities.
  • Two-phase method: (I) capture high-entropy tokens via the shared A matrix in LoRA; (II) perform asymmetric LoRA parameter isolation to unlearn tokens in target subdomains.
  • Reports strong results (≥95% forget quality) on TOFU, WMDP, and MUSE benchmarks while preserving >90% model utility and reducing computational overhead versus full-model updates.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xunlei Chen', 'Jinyu Guo', 'Yuang Li', 'Zhaokun Wang', 'Yi Gong', 'Jie Zou', 'Jiwei Wei', 'Wenhong Tian']
  • Tags: ['unlearning', 'privacy', 'model-editing', 'LoRA', 'parameter-efficient-finetuning']

item.title: Extracting Training Dialogue Data from Large Language Model based Task Bots
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes privacy risks of LLM-based Task-Oriented Dialogue Systems (TODS) with respect to memorization of training dialogue data.
  • Evaluates existing training-data extraction and membership-inference attacks, identifies why prior methods underperform on TODS, and proposes novel attack techniques tailored to LLM-based TODS.
  • Demonstrates empirical effectiveness: extracts thousands of dialogue-state labels with best-case precision >70%, and provides analysis of factors influencing memorization plus targeted mitigation strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shuo Zhang', 'Junzhou Zhao', 'Junji Hou', 'Pinghui Wang', 'Chenxu Wang', 'Jing Tao']
  • Tags: ['training-data-extraction', 'membership-inference', 'privacy', 'task-oriented-dialogue-systems', 'data-memorization']

item.title: Token-level Data Selection for Safe LLM Fine-tuning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies safety degradation during LLM fine-tuning and diagnoses it at token-level rather than sample-level.
  • Proposes TOSS, a token-level data selection framework that scores tokens by loss difference between a safety-degraded model and a utility-oriented model to remove unsafe tokens while preserving task-relevant content.
  • Introduces TOSS-Pro, a progressive refinement that iteratively improves the safety-degraded model for better unsafe-token identification; shows improved safety-utility trade-offs versus sample-level defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanping Li', 'Zhening Liu', 'Zijian Li', 'Zehong Lin', 'Jun Zhang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'data-sanitization', 'fine-tuning', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Constitutional Black-Box Monitoring for Scheming in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes constitutional black-box monitors: prompted LLM classifiers that detect 'scheming' by agents using only external inputs/outputs and synthetic training data.
  • Introduces two synthetic trajectory generation pipelines (STRIDE and Gloom) to produce training datasets and optimizes monitors via prompt sweeps, human refinement, and automated prompt optimization.
  • Evaluates monitors on 7,500 held-out trajectories from ControlArena (grounded agent environments) and finds monitors trained on synthetic data can generalize to more realistic settings.
  • Finds detection performance saturates quickly: simple prompt sweeps match extensive optimization, and further tuning causes overfitting rather than improved robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simon Storf', 'Rich Barton-Cooper', 'James Peters-Gill', 'Marius Hobbhahn']
  • Tags: ['Agent monitoring', 'LLM-based defenses', 'Synthetic datasets', 'Scheming detection']

item.title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of LLM Unlearning Robustness under Multi-Turn Interaction
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates robustness of machine unlearning for LLMs in multi-turn interactive settings, beyond standard static, single-turn evaluations.
  • Evaluates two interaction patterns—self-correction and dialogue-conditioned querying—and shows that ostensibly forgotten knowledge can often be recovered through interaction.
  • Finds that stronger unlearning can produce behavioral rigidity rather than genuine knowledge erasure, and that static evaluations may overestimate real-world forgetting effectiveness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruihao Pan', 'Suhang Wang']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'robustness', 'interactive attacks', 'data removal']

item.title: BLUFF: Benchmarking the Detection of False and Synthetic Content across 58 Low-Resource Languages
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces BLUFF, a large multilingual benchmark (202K+ samples across 79 languages) for detecting false and LLM-synthetic content, with emphasis on low-resource languages.
  • Presents AXL-CoI, a multi-agent adversarial generation framework, and mPURIFY, a quality-filtering pipeline; dataset includes human-written, LLM-generated, translated, and hybrid texts plus 39 manipulation techniques and varying edit intensities.
  • Evaluates state-of-the-art detectors and shows substantial performance degradation (up to 25.3% F1) on low-resource languages, providing dataset, code, and analyses to improve multilingual falsehood/synthetic content detection.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jason Lucas', 'Matt Murtagh-White', 'Adaku Uchendu', 'Ali Al-Lawati', 'Michiharu Yamashita', 'Dominik Macko', 'Ivan Srba', 'Robert Moro', 'Dongwon Lee']
  • Tags: ['misinformation-detection', 'synthetic-text-detection', 'adversarial-generation', 'multilingual', 'benchmark']

item.title: Policy Compliance of User Requests in Natural Language for AI Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the first benchmark of annotated natural-language user requests labeled for compliance with a diverse set of organizational policies.
  • Frames policy compliance assessment as a safety/guardrail problem for AI systems that fulfill user requests.
  • Evaluates multiple LLMs and solution methods on the benchmark, analyzing performance differences and highlighting the difficulty of reliable policy enforcement.
  • Provides empirical insights useful for developing automated policy-checking defenses and safety evaluation pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pedro Cisneros-Velarde']
  • Tags: ['policy compliance', 'safety/guardrails', 'benchmark', 'LLM evaluation']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JAILBREAK FOUNDRY (JBF), a system that translates jailbreak papers into runnable attack modules via a multi-agent workflow with three components: JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluation).
  • Reproduced 30 jailbreak attacks with high fidelity (mean reproduced–reported ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduced attack-specific implementation code by ~50%, and achieved an 82.5% mean reused-code ratio.
  • Enables standardized, automated AdvBench evaluations across 10 victim models using a consistent GPT-4o judge, supporting living benchmarks and scalable red-teaming/attack benchmarking.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial attacks', 'reproducibility', 'benchmarking', 'automated red teaming']

item.title: GPU-Fuzz: Finding Memory Errors in Deep Learning Frameworks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces GPU-Fuzz, a fuzzer that models operator parameters as formal constraints and uses a constraint solver to generate test cases targeting GPU kernel boundary conditions.
  • Aims to find GPU memory errors in deep learning frameworks that can cause crashes or security issues.
  • Applied GPU-Fuzz to PyTorch, TensorFlow, and PaddlePaddle and discovered 13 previously unknown bugs.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness of constraint-guided fuzzing for locating memory errors in GPU kernels of DL frameworks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zihao Li', 'Hongyi Lu', 'Yanan Guo', 'Zhenkai Zhang', 'Shuai Wang', 'Fengwei Zhang']
  • Tags: ['fuzzing', 'memory-errors', 'deep-learning-frameworks', 'vulnerability-discovery', 'GPU-kernels']

item.title: Towards Transferable Defense Against Malicious Image Edits
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TDAE, a bimodal (image+text) defense framework to make images robust against malicious diffusion-based edits.
  • Introduces FlatGrad Defense Mechanism (FDM) that uses gradient regularization to steer perturbations to flat minima for better cross-model robustness.
  • Proposes Dynamic Prompt Defense (DPD) which iteratively optimizes text embeddings alongside image updates to enforce transferability across unseen editors; shows state-of-the-art intra- and cross-model mitigation in experiments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jie Zhang', 'Shuai Dong', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'adversarial robustness', 'diffusion models', 'prompt defense', 'transferability']

item.title: TAO: Tolerance-Aware Optimistic Verification for Floating-Point Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TAO, a tolerance-aware optimistic verification protocol that accepts outputs within operator-level acceptance regions rather than requiring bitwise equality for floating-point neural network execution on untrusted hardware.
  • Combines two error models: sound per-operator IEEE-754 worst-case bounds and tight empirical percentile profiles calibrated across hardware; discrepancies trigger a Merkle-anchored, threshold-guided dispute game that recursively isolates offending operators.
  • Implements TAO as a PyTorch-compatible runtime and blockchain-backed contract layer; evaluates across CNNs, Transformers, and diffusion models on multiple GPUs showing empirical thresholds much tighter than theoretical bounds and negligible runtime overhead, preventing bound-aware adversarial attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianzhu Yao', 'Hongxu Su', 'Taobo Liao', 'Zerui Cheng', 'Huan Zhang', 'Xuechao Wang', 'Pramod Viswanath']
  • Tags: ['model verification', 'integrity auditing', 'robustness', 'ML-as-a-Service', 'adversarial defenses']

item.title: Language steering in latent space to mitigate unintended code-switching
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a lightweight inference-time latent-space language steering method: identify language directions via PCA on parallel translations and shift token embeddings along these axes to control language identity.
  • Requires minimal parallel data for calibration and negligible computational overhead, using a single principal component to achieve 95–99% language classification accuracy.
  • Empirical results on Qwen2.5 and Llama-3.2 show up to 55% reduction in next-token distributional divergence and 63–99% reduction in a Generation Code-Switching Index across multiple language pairs (statistically significant).
  • Layer-wise analysis reveals language identity concentrates in final layers with near-perfect linear separability, informing where steering is most effective.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Andrey Goncharov', 'Nikolai Kondusov', 'Alexey Zaytsev']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'multilingual', 'code-switching mitigation', 'latent-space steering']

item.title: Train Once, Answer All: Many Pretraining Experiments for the Cost of One
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a method to run multiple controlled pretraining experiments concurrently in a single training run, enabling many experiments for the compute cost of one.
  • Replicates prior work on data contamination, data poisoning, and memorization, and runs new experiments on knowledge acquisition, mathematical reasoning, and watermarking.
  • Proposes continual pretraining dependence testing (CPDT) to detect interactions between simultaneous experiments and finds interactions negligible in their setup.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sebastian Bordt', 'Martin Pawelczyk']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'memorization/privacy', 'data-contamination', 'watermarking/defense', 'pretraining-methodology']

item.title: BinaryShield: Cross-Service Threat Intelligence in LLM Services using Privacy-Preserving Fingerprints
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BinaryShield, a privacy-preserving threat intelligence system that enables sharing of attack fingerprints across compliance boundaries to mitigate prompt injection risk.
  • Transforms suspicious prompts via PII redaction, semantic embedding, binary quantization, and randomized response to produce privacy-preserving fingerprints that retain attack patterns.
  • Evaluates effectiveness and efficiency: achieves F1=0.94 (vs SimHash 0.77), reduces storage, and enables ~38x faster similarity search than dense embeddings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Waris Gill', 'Natalie Isak', 'Matthew Dressman']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'privacy-preserving', 'threat intelligence', 'attack detection']

item.title: Control Tax: The Price of Keeping AI in Check
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a theoretical framework that quantifies 'Control Tax' — the operational and financial cost of integrating oversight/control measures and links classifier performance to safety guarantees.
  • Empirically evaluates state-of-the-art language models in adversarial settings where attacker models insert subtle backdoors into code and monitoring models attempt to detect these vulnerabilities.
  • Provides empirical financial cost estimates for control protocols and designs optimized monitoring strategies that trade off safety and cost under practical constraints (e.g., auditing budgets).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mikhail Terekhov', 'Zhen Ning David Liu', 'Caglar Gulcehre', 'Samuel Albanie']
  • Tags: ['backdoor attacks', 'detection/monitoring', 'operational cost / economics', 'adversarial evaluation', 'AI governance/control']

item.title: Back to Square Roots: An Optimal Bound on the Matrix Factorization Error for Multi-Epoch Differentially Private SGD
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Banded Inverse Square Root (BISR), an explicit matrix factorization method that imposes a banded structure on the inverse correlation matrix to handle repeated participation in multi-epoch DP-SGD.
  • Derives an explicit and tight characterization of multi-epoch factorization error and proves that BISR is asymptotically optimal by matching upper and lower bounds.
  • Empirically shows BISR matches state-of-the-art factorization methods while being simpler, computationally efficient, and easier to analyze.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nikita P. Kalinin', 'Ryan McKenna', 'Jalaj Upadhyay', 'Christoph H. Lampert']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'DP-SGD', 'privacy-preserving ML', 'matrix factorization', 'multi-epoch privacy accounting']

item.title: Sparsification Under Siege: Dual-Level Defense Against Poisoning in Communication-Efficient Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a 'sparsity-robustness trade-off' where gradient sparsification in federated learning creates geometric properties (high-dimensional orthogonality) that allow poisoning attacks to evade Euclidean-based robust aggregation.
  • Proposes SafeSparse, a dual-level defense that (1) Structure-Aware Calibration uses Jaccard similarity to detect/filter topological/index-poisoning outliers and (2) Directional Semantic Alignment applies density-based clustering on update signs to mitigate magnitude-invariant attacks.
  • Provides theoretical convergence guarantees for the proposed method and extensive experiments showing up to 25.7% recovery in global accuracy under coordinated poisoning scenarios.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhiyong Jin', 'Runhua Xu', 'Chao Li', 'Yizhong Liu', 'Jianxin Li', 'James Joshi']
  • Tags: ['federated-learning', 'poisoning', 'robust-aggregation', 'sparsification', 'defense']

item.title: Safety Mirage: How Spurious Correlations Undermine VLM Safety Fine-Tuning and Can Be Mitigated by Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a 'safety mirage' where supervised safety fine-tuning for vision-language models (VLMs) induces spurious text-safety correlations rather than robust mitigation.
  • Shows a practical one-word modification attack that exploits these spurious correlations to bypass safety guards and also causes over-prudence (unnecessary refusals).
  • Proposes machine unlearning (MU) as an alternative alignment method that removes harmful knowledge while preserving general capabilities.
  • Evaluations show MU-based alignment can reduce attack success rates by up to 60.27% and cut unnecessary rejections by over 84.20% across safety benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yiwei Chen', 'Yuguang Yao', 'Yihua Zhang', 'Bingquan Shen', 'Gaowen Liu', 'Sijia Liu']
  • Tags: ['VLM safety', 'spurious correlations', 'adversarial attack', 'machine unlearning', 'alignment']

item.title: Support Tokens, Stability Margins, and a New Foundation for Robust LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Reformulates causal self-attention in transformers within a probabilistic/Bayesian framework, revealing a barrier constraint on attention parameters that induces structured geometry in token space.
  • Identifies a boundary where attention becomes ill-conditioned and interprets this as a stability margin analogous to support vectors, introducing the notion of "support tokens."
  • Derives a MAP training objective that augments cross-entropy with a smooth log-barrier penalty (minimal change to standard training) to enforce the constraint.
  • Empirically shows the proposed regularization yields more robust models without sacrificing out-of-sample accuracy and is straightforward to incorporate.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Deepak Agarwal', 'Dhyey Dharmendrakumar Mavani', 'Suyash Gupta', 'Karthik Sethuraman', 'Tejas Dharamsi']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'training-regularization', 'model-stability', 'self-attention', 'probabilistic-framework']

item.title: The Power of Decaying Steps: Enhancing Attack Stability and Transferability for Sign-based Optimizers
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes instability and non-convergence in sign-based adversarial optimizers (e.g., I-FGSM, MI-FGSM) by reformulating them as coordinate-wise gradient descent and identifying non-decaying step-sizes as a key cause.
  • Proposes Monotonically Decreasing Coordinate-wise Step-sizes (MDCS) for sign-based optimizers and introduces MDCS-MI with a theoretical convergence rate of O(1/√T).
  • Empirically demonstrates that MDCS variants improve attack stability and cross-model transferability on image classification and cross-modal retrieval benchmarks compared to state-of-the-art sign-based attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wei Tao', 'Yang Dai', 'Jincai Huang', 'Qing Tao']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'sign-based-optimizers', 'attack-transferability', 'optimization-theory']

item.title: Agentic Unlearning: When LLM Agent Meets Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces 'agentic unlearning' to remove specified sensitive information from both model parameters and persistent agent memory to prevent reactivation via closed-loop interactions.
  • Proposes Synchronized Backflow Unlearning (SBU) combining dependency-closure memory pruning and stochastic reference alignment for parametric suppression, coordinated via a dual-update protocol to avoid cross-pathway recontamination.
  • Evaluates on medical QA benchmarks, reporting reduced traces of targeted private information across both memory and parameters with limited utility degradation on retained data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bin Wang', 'Fan Wang', 'Pingping Wang', 'Jinyu Cong', 'Yang Yu', 'Yilong Yin', 'Zhongyi Han', 'Benzheng Wei']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'model defense', 'LLM agents', 'memory safety']

item.title: Stealthy Poisoning Attacks Bypass Defenses in Regression Settings
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an optimal stealthy poisoning attack formulation for regression models that models varying degrees of detectability and demonstrates that these attacks can bypass state-of-the-art defenses.
  • Introduces a normalization-based methodology to evaluate trade-offs between attack effectiveness and detectability.
  • Develops a novel defense, BayesClean, which improves robustness against stealthy poisoning when attacks are subtle and the number of poisoned points is significant.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Javier Carnerero-Cano', "Luis Mu\\~noz-Gonz\\'alez", 'Phillippa Spencer', 'Emil C. Lupu']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'poisoning-attacks', 'defenses', 'stealthy-attacks', 'regression-robustness']

item.title: GNN Explanations that do not Explain and How to find Them
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a critical failure mode in self-explainable GNNs (SE-GNNs): explanations can be unambiguously unrelated to how the model actually infers labels (degenerate explanations).
  • Shows SE-GNNs can achieve optimal predictive risk while producing degenerate explanations and that common faithfulness metrics often fail to detect this.
  • Demonstrates degenerate explanations can be maliciously planted to hide use of sensitive attributes and can also arise naturally, motivating the need for auditing.
  • Proposes a novel faithfulness metric that reliably flags degenerate explanations in both adversarial and natural settings, with empirical validation and code release.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Steve Azzolin', 'Stefano Teso', 'Bruno Lepri', 'Andrea Passerini', 'Sagar Malhotra']
  • Tags: ['graph-neural-networks', 'explainability', 'adversarial-manipulation', 'privacy', 'audit']

item.title: HalluGuard: Demystifying Data-Driven and Reasoning-Driven Hallucinations in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a theoretical Hallucination Risk Bound that decomposes hallucination risk into data-driven (training-time mismatch) and reasoning-driven (inference-time instability) components.
  • Proposes HalluGuard, an NTK-based scoring method that leverages Neural Tangent Kernel geometry to jointly detect both types of hallucinations.
  • Evaluates on 10 benchmarks, compares to 11 baselines across 9 LLM backbones, reporting state-of-the-art hallucination detection performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinyue Zeng', 'Junhong Lin', 'Yujun Yan', 'Feng Guo', 'Liang Shi', 'Jun Wu', 'Dawei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'model safety / guardrails', 'robustness', 'theoretical analysis', 'NTK-based methods']

item.title: Spurious Correlation-Aware Embedding Regularization for Worst-Group Robustness
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SCER (Spurious Correlation-Aware Embedding Regularization), a method that regularizes feature embeddings to suppress spurious cues and improve worst-group robustness.
  • Provides theoretical analysis showing worst-group error depends on classifier reliance on spurious vs. core directions, identified via differences in group-wise mean embeddings.
  • Imposes embedding-level constraints to encourage focus on core features and reduce sensitivity to spurious patterns, and reports empirical SOTA gains on multiple vision and language benchmarks.
  • Code is released (GitHub) and evaluations span both vision and NLP settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subeen Park', 'Joowang Kim', 'Hakyung Lee', 'Sunjae Yoo', 'Kyungwoo Song']
  • Tags: ['worst-group-robustness', 'spurious-correlations', 'representation-regularization', 'distribution-shift', 'robustness']

item.title: Adversarial D\'ej\`a Vu: Jailbreak Dictionary Learning for Stronger Generalization to Unseen Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the "Adversarial Déjà Vu" hypothesis: novel jailbreaks are largely recombinations of previously seen adversarial skills.
  • Analyzes 32 jailbreak/attack papers with an automated pipeline to extract and compress adversarial skills into a sparse, human-readable dictionary of primitives.
  • Introduces Adversarial Skill Compositional Training (ASCoT), which trains models on diverse compositions of skill primitives to improve robustness to unseen and multi-turn jailbreaks while maintaining low over-refusal.
  • Finds that expanding adversarial skill coverage (the diversity of primitives) is more important than just increasing data scale for defending against novel attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mahavir Dabas', 'Tran Huynh', 'Nikhil Reddy Billa', 'Jiachen T. Wang', 'Peng Gao', 'Charith Peris', 'Yao Ma', 'Rahul Gupta', 'Ming Jin', 'Prateek Mittal', 'Ruoxi Jia']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial training', 'red teaming', 'robustness', 'dictionary learning']

item.title: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes adaptive attacks by untrusted LLMs that know the AI control protocol and monitor model, enabling prompt-injection style payloads embedded in model outputs.
  • Empirically demonstrates that such adaptive attacks consistently evade diverse LLM monitors and complete malicious tasks on two AI control benchmarks.
  • Shows that protocols relying on monitors (including Defer-to-Resample) are fundamentally vulnerable; resampling can amplify injections and act as a best-of-n attack.
  • Argues that adaptive attacks on monitor models are a major blind spot and should be a standard part of evaluations for future AI control mechanisms.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mikhail Terekhov', 'Alexander Panfilov', 'Daniil Dzenhaliou', 'Caglar Gulcehre', 'Maksym Andriushchenko', 'Ameya Prabhu', 'Jonas Geiping']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'monitor evasion', 'red teaming', 'adversarial attacks', 'AI control protocols']

item.title: Robust Fine-Tuning from Non-Robust Pretrained Models: Mitigating Suboptimal Transfer With Epsilon-Scheduling
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'suboptimal transfer': fine-tuning non-robust pretrained models with a robust objective can impede task adaptation and yield poor adversarial robustness/accuracy.
  • Proposes Epsilon-Scheduling, a heuristic schedule over perturbation strength during training to avoid the transfer failure and enable effective robust fine-tuning (RFT).
  • Introduces 'expected robustness' as a metric to evaluate performance across a range of perturbation strengths, capturing the accuracy–robustness trade-off.
  • Provides extensive experiments (six pretrained models, five datasets) showing Epsilon-Scheduling consistently prevents suboptimal transfer and improves expected robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Jonas Ngnaw\\'e", 'Maxime Heuillet', 'Sabyasachi Sahoo', 'Yann Pequignot', 'Ola Ahmad', 'Audrey Durand', "Fr\\'ed\\'eric Precioso", "Christian Gagn\\'e"]
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'robust fine-tuning', 'adversarial training', 'transfer learning', 'defense']

item.title: Chasing the Tail: Effective Rubric-based Reward Modeling for Large Language Model Post-Training
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies reward over-optimization in reinforcement fine-tuning as arising from reward misspecification concentrated in the high-reward tail (difficulty distinguishing Excellent vs Great responses).
  • Proposes rubric-based reward modeling that can leverage off-policy exemplars (stronger models or rewrites) while being robust to their artifacts, focusing on discriminating diverse high-quality responses.
  • Introduces a workflow to elicit tail-focused rubrics and shows empirically that rubric-based rewards mitigate reward hacking and improve post-training LLM performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junkai Zhang', 'Zihao Wang', 'Lin Gui', 'Swarnashree Mysore Sathyendra', 'Jaehwan Jeong', 'Victor Veitch', 'Wei Wang', 'Yunzhong He', 'Bing Liu', 'Lifeng Jin']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'robustness/defense', 'reward modeling', 'alignment', 'reinforcement fine-tuning']

item.title: FORCE: Transferable Visual Jailbreaking Attacks via Feature Over-Reliance CorrEction
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes why simple visual jailbreaking attacks on multimodal LLMs have poor cross-model transferability, attributing it to attacks lying in high-sharpness regions of the loss landscape.
  • Identifies feature over-reliance in both intermediate layer representations and spectral (frequency) components—attacks exploit narrow layer features and semantically poor frequencies that do not generalize.
  • Proposes FORCE (Feature Over-Reliance CorrEction), which encourages exploration across broader layer features and rescales frequency influences by semantic content to flatten feasible regions for attacks.
  • Empirical results show FORCE produces more transferable visual jailbreaking attacks, improving visual red-teaming capability against closed-source MLLMs.
