Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Runqi Lin', 'Alasdair Paren', 'Suqin Yuan', 'Muyang Li', 'Philip Torr', 'Adel Bibi', 'Tongliang Liu']
  • Tags: ['visual-jailbreaking', 'transferability', 'adversarial-attacks', 'multimodal-LLMs', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Gaming and Cooperation in Federated Learning: What Can Happen and How to Monitor It
item.description:
Summary

  • Models federated learning deployments as strategic systems and distinguishes welfare-improving cooperation from metric gaming by participants.
  • Introduces indices to quantify manipulability, price of gaming, and price of cooperation, and derives threshold conditions to deter harmful gaming while preserving benign cooperation.
  • Proposes monitoring and governance tools: early-warning indicators, auto-switching aggregator rules, a governance checklist, and an audit-budget allocation algorithm with provable performance guarantees.
  • Validates theoretical predictions via simulations across stylized environments and a federated learning case study.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dongseok Kim', 'Hyoungsun Choi', 'Mohamed Jismy Aashik Rasool', 'Gisung Oh']
  • Tags: ['federated learning', 'metric gaming', 'incentives and manipulation', 'monitoring and auditing', 'defense mechanisms']

item.title: SecP-Tuning: Efficient Privacy-Preserving Prompt Tuning for Large Language Models via MPC
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SecP-Tuning, an MPC-based framework for privacy-preserving prompt tuning of large language models that avoids expensive MPC-backed backward propagation by using a Forward-only Tuning (FoT) data owner–server interaction paradigm.
  • Introduces a privacy-preserving Random Feature Attention (RFA) to replace softmax-based self-attention, eliminating MPC-incompatible nonlinearities and reducing computational/communication costs.
  • Reports substantial efficiency gains (≈12x–16x end-to-end speedup and ≈17x–20x communication reduction) over full-parameter supervised fine-tuning and gradient-based prompt tuning, while retaining comparable few-shot task performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jinglong Luo', 'Zhuo Zhang', 'Yehong Zhang', 'Shiyu Liu', 'Ye Dong', 'Hui Wang', 'Yue Yu', 'Xun Zhou', 'Zenglin Xu']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving ML', 'secure multi-party computation (MPC)', 'prompt tuning', 'efficient/robust training']

item.title: GradPCA: Leveraging NTK Alignment for Reliable Out-of-Distribution Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces GradPCA, an OOD detection method that applies PCA to class-mean gradients leveraging low-rank structure from NTK alignment.
  • Provides theoretical analysis linking spectral OOD detection effectiveness to feature-space properties that emerge under NTK alignment and the role of pretrained representations.
  • Empirically demonstrates more consistent OOD detection performance across standard image classification benchmarks compared to existing methods.
  • Offers guidance for designing spectral OOD detectors and highlights that feature quality (pretrained vs non-pretrained) crucially impacts detector success.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mariia Seleznova', 'Hung-Hsu Chou', 'Claudio Mayrink Verdun', 'Gitta Kutyniok']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution detection', 'robustness', 'Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK)', 'gradient-based detection', 'spectral methods / PCA']

item.title: Adversarially Pretrained Transformers May Be Universally Robust In-Context Learners
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents theoretical analysis showing adversarially pretrained transformers can act as universally robust foundation models that provide robust in-context learning on unseen classification tasks without further adversarial training.
  • Shows single-layer linear transformers, adversarially pretrained across diverse tasks, can generalize robustly from clean demonstrations by focusing on robust features.
  • Identifies two practical challenges: the accuracy–robustness trade-off and high sample complexity during adversarial pretraining.
  • Provides code and positions adversarial pretraining as a costly but potentially high-leverage defense that yields robustness for downstream tasks 'for free.'

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Soichiro Kumano', 'Hiroshi Kera', 'Toshihiko Yamasaki']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'adversarial training', 'in-context learning', 'foundation models', 'robustness theory']

item.title: Self-Destructive Language Model
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SEAM, a defense that makes LLMs 'self-destructive' by preserving benign capabilities while causing performance collapse when fine-tuned on harmful data.
  • Mechanism: a novel loss that couples optimization trajectories for benign and harmful data, augmented with adversarial gradient ascent to amplify the self-destructive effect.
  • Provides a practical training procedure with an efficient Hessian-free gradient estimator and theoretical error bounds; evaluates across models and datasets showing strong robustness to low-intensity attacks and catastrophic collapse under high-intensity attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhui Wang', 'Rongyi Zhu', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['fine-tuning attacks', 'defense', 'adversarial training', 'model robustness']

item.title: Aggressive or Imperceptible, or Both: Network Pruning Assisted Hybrid Byzantines in Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a hybrid Byzantine attack for federated learning that leverages NN structure and sparsity to be both aggressive and stealthy.
  • Attack has two coordinated components: a sparse, high-impact perturbation targeting sensitive parameters, and a slow-accumulating component that poisons parameters over multiple rounds to evade detection.
  • Demonstrates the attack's effectiveness against eight state-of-the-art Byzantine-robust aggregation defenses via extensive simulations.
  • Highlights the limitation of outlier-detection defenses that ignore internal network structure and shows how pruning/sensitivity information can be exploited by adversaries.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Emre Ozfatura', 'Kerem Ozfatura', 'Baturalp Buyukates', 'Mert Coskuner', 'Alptekin Kupcu', 'Deniz Gunduz']
  • Tags: ['federated-learning', 'Byzantine-attacks', 'model-poisoning', 'network-pruning', 'stealthy-attacks']

item.title: Conformal Policy Control
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes 'Conformal Policy Control': use any safe reference policy as a probabilistic regulator to limit how aggressively a new (optimized/untested) policy can act.
  • Uses conformal calibration on data from the safe policy to enforce a user-declared risk tolerance with finite-sample guarantees, including for non-monotonic bounded constraint functions.
  • Does not require correct model class specification or hyperparameter tuning, and enables safe exploration from first deployment.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness across tasks (e.g., natural language question answering and biomolecular engineering) where safe exploration can also improve performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Drew Prinster', 'Clara Fannjiang', 'Ji Won Park', 'Kyunghyun Cho', 'Anqi Liu', 'Suchi Saria', 'Samuel Stanton']
  • Tags: ['safe exploration', 'conformal calibration', 'policy safety', 'provable guarantees', 'risk-aware control']

item.title: From Leaderboard to Deployment: Code Quality Challenges in AV Perception Repositories
item.description:
Summary

  • Large-scale empirical study of software quality in 178 AV perception repositories from KITTI and NuScenes 3D object detection leaderboards.
  • Used static analysis tools (Pylint, Bandit, Radon) to evaluate code errors, high-severity security vulnerabilities, maintainability, and development practices.
  • Found only 7.3% of repositories met basic production-readiness criteria (no critical errors and no high-severity vulnerabilities); security issues concentrated in a few common problem types.
  • Correlated CI/CD adoption with better maintainability and proposed actionable guidelines to prevent the most common security issues.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mateus Karvat', 'Bram Adams', 'Sidney Givigi']
  • Tags: ['software security', 'vulnerability analysis', 'static analysis', 'autonomous vehicles', 'production readiness']

item.title: What Helps -- and What Hurts: Bidirectional Explanations for Vision Transformers
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BiCAM, a bidirectional class activation mapping that preserves signed (positive and negative) attributions for Vision Transformers.
  • Introduces a Positive-to-Negative Ratio (PNR) that summarizes attribution balance and can be used for lightweight detection of adversarial examples without retraining.
  • Demonstrates improved localization and faithfulness on ImageNet, VOC, and COCO and generalization across ViT variants (DeiT, Swin).
  • Primary contribution is interpretability, with an ancillary application to adversarial example detection (defensive/detection mechanism).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qin Su', 'Tie Luo']
  • Tags: ['explainability', 'vision-transformers', 'adversarial-detection', 'class-activation-mapping', 'robustness']

item.title: Inference-Time Safety For Code LLMs Via Retrieval-Augmented Revision
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an inference-time safety mechanism for code LLMs that uses retrieval-augmented generation to surface security risks and guide automated code revision using curated Stack Overflow security discussions.
  • Design emphasizes interpretability (transparent interventions grounded in expert community explanations), robustness to evolving vulnerabilities without retraining, and real-time safety alignment prior to deployment.
  • Empirical evaluation shows improved security of LLM-generated code vs. prompting alone and no introduction of new vulnerabilities per static analysis across benchmarks and real-world datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Manisha Mukherjee', 'Vincent J. Hellendoorn']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'safe-code-generation', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'inference-time-mitigation', 'security-evaluation']

item.title: A SUPERB-Style Benchmark of Self-Supervised Speech Models for Audio Deepfake Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Spoof-SUPERB, a SUPERB-style benchmark evaluating 20 self-supervised speech (SSL) models for audio deepfake (spoof) detection across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.
  • Finds large-scale discriminative SSL models (e.g., XLS-R, UniSpeech-SAT, WavLM Large) consistently outperform generative and spectrogram-based models, attributed to multilingual pretraining, speaker-aware objectives, and model scale.
  • Analyzes robustness under acoustic degradations, showing generative approaches degrade sharply while discriminative models remain more resilient, and provides a reproducible baseline for securing speech systems against deepfakes.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hashim Ali', 'Nithin Sai Adupa', 'Surya Subramani', 'Hafiz Malik']
  • Tags: ['audio-deepfake-detection', 'spoofing', 'benchmark', 'self-supervised-learning', 'robustness']

item.title: Beyond Reward: A Bounded Measure of Agent Environment Coupling
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces bipredictability (P), a bounded information-theoretic measure of agent–environment coupling computed from the observation–action–outcome loop.
  • P is estimated in real time via an auxiliary monitor called the Information Digital Twin (IDT), which also provides diagnostic components for interaction effectiveness.
  • Evaluated on SAC and PPO in MuJoCo HalfCheetah under 168 perturbation trials: IDT detected 89.3% of perturbations vs 44.0% for reward-based monitoring and had 4.4x lower median detection latency; nominal P reported as 0.33 ± 0.02.
  • Claims bipredictability enables earlier detection of interaction degradation (before performance drops) and can serve as a prerequisite signal for closed-loop self-regulation in deployed RL systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wael Hafez', 'Cameron Reid', 'Amit Nazeri']
  • Tags: ['reinforcement learning', 'robustness', 'runtime monitoring', 'anomaly detection', 'distribution shift']

item.title: Can AI Agents Agree?
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates LLM-based agents on a synchronous all-to-all Byzantine consensus game over scalar values, focusing on agreement (not value optimality) in a no-stake setting.
  • Runs hundreds of simulations across model sizes, group sizes, and fractions of Byzantine agents, finding valid agreement is unreliable and degrades with larger groups.
  • Shows that introducing a small number of Byzantine agents substantially reduces success, with failures dominated by loss of liveness (timeouts, stalled convergence) rather than subtle value corruption.
  • Concludes that reliable agreement is not yet an emergent capability of current LLM-agent groups, raising caution for deployments that require robust coordination.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Fr\\'ed\\'eric Berdoz", 'Leonardo Rugli', 'Roger Wattenhofer']
  • Tags: ['Byzantine consensus', 'LLM robustness', 'multi-agent security', 'adversarial agents', 'distributed coordination']

item.title: Turning Black Box into White Box: Dataset Distillation Leaks
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that dataset distillation can leak sensitive information because synthetic datasets encode model weight trajectories and become over-informative.
  • Introduces the Information Revelation Attack (IRA) that infers the distillation algorithm and model architecture, performs membership inference, and recovers sensitive training samples.
  • Empirical results demonstrate successful privacy attacks against state-of-the-art distillation methods, exposing significant privacy vulnerabilities in distilled datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huajie Chen', 'Tianqing Zhu', 'Yuchen Zhong', 'Yang Zhang', 'Shang Wang', 'Feng He', 'Lefeng Zhang', 'Jialiang Shen', 'Minghao Wang', 'Wanlei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['dataset-distillation', 'privacy-leakage', 'membership-inference', 'attack', 'model-inference']

item.title: BadRSSD: Backdoor Attacks on Regularized Self-Supervised Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BadRSSD, a novel backdoor attack that targets the representation layer of self-supervised diffusion models rather than their generative outputs.
  • Hijacks semantic representations in PCA space for poisoned samples and applies coordinated constraints across latent, pixel, and feature-distribution spaces to steer denoising toward a specified target.
  • Introduces representation dispersion regularization to preserve feature-space uniformity and stealth, maintaining normal model utility while enabling precise triggered target generation.
  • Empirical results show Strong performance (FID/MSE) across benchmarks, architectures, and robustness against state-of-the-art backdoor defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiayao Wang', 'Yiping Zhang', 'Mohammad Maruf Hasan', 'Xiaoying Lei', 'Jiale Zhang', 'Junwu Zhu', 'Qilin Wu', 'Dongfang Zhao']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'diffusion models', 'self-supervised learning', 'representation-layer attack', 'model security']

item.title: Constitutional Black-Box Monitoring for Scheming in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes constitutional black-box monitors: prompted LLM classifiers that detect 'scheming' by agents using only external inputs/outputs and synthetic training data.
  • Introduces two synthetic trajectory generation pipelines (STRIDE and Gloom) to produce training datasets and optimizes monitors via prompt sweeps, human refinement, and automated prompt optimization.
  • Evaluates monitors on 7,500 held-out trajectories from ControlArena (grounded agent environments) and finds monitors trained on synthetic data can generalize to more realistic settings.
  • Finds detection performance saturates quickly: simple prompt sweeps match extensive optimization, and further tuning causes overfitting rather than improved robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simon Storf', 'Rich Barton-Cooper', 'James Peters-Gill', 'Marius Hobbhahn']
  • Tags: ['Agent monitoring', 'LLM-based defenses', 'Synthetic datasets', 'Scheming detection']

item.title: Multimodal Adaptive Retrieval Augmented Generation through Internal Representation Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Multimodal Adaptive RAG (MMA-RAG), which dynamically decides whether to incorporate retrieved external evidence based on model-internal confidence to reduce hallucinations in visual question answering.
  • Implements a decision classifier trained via layer-wise analysis that leverages joint internal visual and textual representations to guide reverse image retrieval and adaptive use of external knowledge.
  • Reports significant performance improvements on three VQA datasets and ablation studies showing the importance of internal representations for retrieval decisions.
  • Emphasizes balancing external knowledge utilization with inference robustness in multimodal retrieval-augmented generation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruoshuang Du', 'Xin Sun', 'Qiang Liu', 'Bowen Song', 'Zhongqi Chen', 'Weiqiang Wang', 'Liang Wang']
  • Tags: ['multimodal RAG', 'hallucination mitigation', 'robustness', 'retrieval decision', 'visual question answering']

item.title: Neurosymbolic Learning for Advanced Persistent Threat Detection under Extreme Class Imbalance
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a neurosymbolic architecture combining an optimized BERT encoder with Logic Tensor Networks (LTN) to provide explainable detection of advanced persistent threats (APTs) in wireless IoT networks.
  • Introduces an efficient feature-to-sequence encoding preserving temporal dependencies, and addresses extreme class imbalance via focal loss, hierarchical classification (normal vs. attack then attack type), and adaptive sampling.
  • Evaluated on the SCVIC-APT2021 dataset, reporting a binary F1 of 95.27% (FPR 0.14%) and a multi-class macro F1 of 76.75%.
  • Includes a statistical explainability analysis that identifies and validates important network features for operational interpretability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Quhura Fathima', 'Neda Moghim', 'Mostafa Taghizade Firouzjaee', 'Christo K. Thomas', 'Ross Gore', 'Walid Saad']
  • Tags: ['APT detection', 'neurosymbolic', 'IoT security', 'class imbalance', 'explainability']

item.title: Detecting Cognitive Signatures in Typing Behavior for Non-Intrusive Authorship Verification
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Cognitive Load Correlation (CLC), a keystroke-timing based signature to distinguish genuine composition from mechanical transcription for authorship verification.
  • Uses large-scale keystroke dataset (136M+ events) and a non-intrusive framework that collects only timing metadata to preserve privacy.
  • Reports analytical discrimination accuracy of ~85–95% under stated assumptions and adopts evidence quantization to limit biometric leakage.
  • Analyzes adversarial robustness, arguing cognitive signatures resist timing-forgery attacks because the cognitive channel is entangled with semantic content.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Condrey']
  • Tags: ['authorship-verification', 'behavioral-biometrics', 'keystroke-dynamics', 'adversarial-robustness', 'privacy-preserving']

item.title: Steering Away from Memorization: Reachability-Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-Image Diffusion
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses memorization (training-data leakage) in text-to-image diffusion models and limitations of prior mitigation methods that degrade quality or alignment.
  • Proposes Reachability-Aware Diffusion Steering (RADS): models the diffusion denoising process as a dynamical system, approximates a backward reachable tube of states that lead to memorized outputs, and uses constrained reinforcement learning to minimally perturb caption embeddings at inference time to avoid those states.
  • Demonstrates improved trade-offs between generation diversity (SSCD), quality (FID), and alignment (CLIP) versus baselines, offering a plug-and-play defense that does not modify the diffusion backbone.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sathwik Karnik', 'Juyeop Kim', 'Sanmi Koyejo', 'Jong-Seok Lee', 'Somil Bansal']
  • Tags: ['memorization', 'privacy', 'defense', 'diffusion models', 'reinforcement learning']

item.title: The Hidden Costs of Domain Fine-Tuning: Pii-Bearing Data Degrades Safety and Increases Leakage
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical study of how domain fine-tuning on booking-support dialogs (5,000 pairs) affects safety and privacy in open-source chat models up to 8B parameters.
  • Finds fine-tuning shifts models from refusals toward harmful compliance on adversarial safety prompts (SORRY-Bench), with the worst degradation when PII is present in the fine-tuning data.
  • Demonstrates PII-bearing fine-tuning increases sensitive identifier leakage in unrelated/out-of-domain queries; role-swapping (RS) reduces leakage somewhat but does not reliably restore refusal behavior.
  • Evaluates out-of-domain behavior (philosophical questions) and provides controlled comparisons across three training configurations: NoPII-NoRS, PII-NoRS, and PII-RS.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jayesh Choudhari', 'Piyush Kumar Singh']
  • Tags: ['privacy leakage', 'PII leakage', 'fine-tuning risks', 'safety degradation', 'adversarial evaluation']

item.title: On the Rate of Convergence of GD in Non-linear Neural Networks: An Adversarial Robustness Perspective
item.description:
Summary

  • Provides a theoretical analysis of gradient descent convergence in a minimal two-neuron ReLU binary classifier, focusing on the evolution of the adversarial robustness margin.
  • Proves GD converges to the optimal robustness margin but at a very slow rate, Theta(1 / ln(t)), establishing an explicit lower bound on robustness margin convergence in a non-linear model.
  • Supports theory with empirical simulations showing the same tight convergence rate across multiple initializations and activation-pattern regimes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Guy Smorodinsky', 'Sveta Gimpleson', 'Itay Safran']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'training dynamics', 'optimization convergence', 'theoretical analysis']

item.title: Explanation-Guided Adversarial Training for Robust and Interpretable Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Explanation-Guided Adversarial Training (EGAT), which integrates adversarial training with explanation-guided learning to improve prediction accuracy, adversarial robustness, and explanation quality simultaneously.
  • EGAT generates adversarial examples during training while imposing constraints on model attributions to enforce attributional stability and semantically meaningful explanations.
  • Provides PAC-style theoretical analysis showing improved stability under unexpected inputs versus standard adversarial training, and reports empirical gains (≈+37% adversarial accuracy, modest +16% training time) on OOD benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chao Chen', 'Yanhui Chen', 'Shanshan Lin', 'Dongsheng Hong', 'Shu Wu', 'Xiangwen Liao', 'Chuanyi Liu']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-training', 'adversarial-robustness', 'explainability', 'attributional-stability', 'OOD-robustness']

item.title: SafeSci: Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models in Science Domains and Beyond
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SafeSci, a comprehensive framework for LLM safety in scientific domains consisting of SafeSciBench (0.25M samples) and SafeSciTrain (1.5M samples) for evaluation and safety enhancement.
  • Uses objective metrics (deterministically answerable questions) to reduce evaluation bias and distinguishes safety knowledge from risk to broaden coverage.
  • Evaluates 24 advanced LLMs, revealing notable safety vulnerabilities and variable excessive refusal behaviors; shows fine-tuning on SafeSciTrain improves safety alignment.
  • Argues safety judgments should be context-dependent rather than universally labeling scientific questions as safe/unsafe, and provides resources for building safer scientific AI systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiangyang Zhu', 'Yuan Tian', 'Qi Jia', 'Kaiwei Zhang', 'Zicheng Zhang', 'Chunyi Li', 'Kaiyuan Ji', 'Dongrui Liu', 'Zijian Chen', 'Lu Sun', 'Renrui Zhang', 'Yan Teng', 'Jing Shao', 'Wei Sun', 'Xia Hu', 'Yu Qiao', 'Guangtao Zhai']
  • Tags: ['safety benchmark', 'model evaluation', 'safety fine-tuning', 'LLM vulnerabilities', 'dataset']

item.title: I Can't Believe It's Not Robust: Catastrophic Collapse of Safety Classifiers under Embedding Drift
item.description:
Summary

  • Small embedding drift (normalized perturbation σ = 0.02, ≈1° angular) causes safety classifiers trained on frozen embeddings to collapse: ROC-AUC falls from 85% to 50%.
  • Failures are often silent: mean confidence only drops ~14%, and 72% of misclassifications occur with high confidence, defeating standard monitoring.
  • Instruction-tuned models exhibit ~20% worse class separability than base models, making aligned systems harder to protect and undermining the assumption that safety checks transfer across model updates.
  • Implication: production safety architectures that rely on frozen-embedding classifiers are fundamentally fragile to embedding drift and need version-robust defenses or continual recalibration.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary', 'Aman Chadha']
  • Tags: ['safety classifiers', 'embedding drift', 'deployment security', 'robustness', 'monitoring failures']

item.title: Integrating LTL Constraints into PPO for Safe Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PPO-LTL: integrates Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) safety constraints into Proximal Policy Optimization for safe reinforcement learning.
  • Uses limit-deterministic Büchi automata to monitor LTL violations, converts violations to penalty costs, and incorporates them via a Lagrangian optimization scheme.
  • Evaluated on Zones and CARLA driving environments, showing reduced safety violations while maintaining competitive task performance; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Maifang Zhang', 'Hang Yu', 'Qian Zuo', 'Cheng Wang', 'Vaishak Belle', 'Fengxiang He']
  • Tags: ['safe reinforcement learning', 'safety constraints', 'Linear Temporal Logic (LTL)', 'PPO', 'formal methods']

item.title: JailNewsBench: Multi-Lingual and Regional Benchmark for Fake News Generation under Jailbreak Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JailNewsBench, a large benchmark to evaluate LLM robustness to jailbreak attacks that induce fake-news generation across languages and regions.
  • Covers 34 regions and 22 languages with ~300k instances, 5 jailbreak attack methods, and 8 evaluation sub-metrics using LLM-as-a-Judge.
  • Evaluates 9 LLMs, reporting up to 86.3% attack success rate and a maximum harmfulness score of 3.5/5; finds notably weaker defenses for English/U.S.-related topics.
  • Shows gaps in existing safety datasets for fake-news coverage and releases dataset and code.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Masahiro Kaneko', 'Ayana Niwa', 'Timothy Baldwin']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak', 'fake-news', 'benchmark', 'LLM-safety', 'multilingual']

item.title: Attention Smoothing Is All You Need For Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Attention Smoothing Unlearning (ASU), which performs unlearning via self-distillation from a forget-teacher by increasing attention softmax temperature to flatten attention distributions.
  • Claims ASU suppresses lexical- and semantic-level associations that enable memorization, producing bounded optimization that erases factual information while preserving coherence.
  • Evaluates on unlearning benchmarks (TOFU, MUSE, WMDP) and real-world/continual unlearning scenarios across question answering and text completion, showing improved forgetting with minimal utility loss.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Saleh Zare Zade', 'Xiangyu Zhou', 'Sijia Liu', 'Dongxiao Zhu']
  • Tags: ['unlearning', 'privacy', 'model editing', 'attention-based defenses', 'self-distillation']

item.title: Subliminal Signals in Preference Labels
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that binary preference labels used in LLM-as-judge oversight can act as a covert communication channel, allowing biased judges to transmit unintended behavioral traits to student models.
  • Demonstrates that these subliminal signals can propagate and amplify across iterative alignment rounds, undermining the assumption that preference labels only convey semantic quality signals.
  • Argues for the need to detect and mitigate subliminal preference transmission in scalable oversight/superalignment settings, especially when judges have misaligned objectives.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Isotta Magistrali', "Fr\\'ed\\'eric Berdoz", 'Sam Dauncey', 'Roger Wattenhofer']
  • Tags: ['label_poisoning', 'covert_communication', 'alignment', 'oversight', 'adversarial_vulnerabilities']

item.title: Compensation-free Machine Unlearning in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models by Eliminating the Mutual Information
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MiM-MU, a compensation-free machine unlearning / concept erasure method for text-to-image diffusion models by minimizing mutual information between the model and an undesired concept.
  • Aims to precisely remove undesired knowledge while preserving generation quality for other concepts without post-hoc compensation or retraining on remaining data.
  • Designs computationally efficient objectives to maintain sampling distribution for non-target concepts and evaluates effectiveness and utility preservation in diffusion models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinwen Cheng', 'Jingyuan Zhang', 'Zhehao Huang', 'Yingwen Wu', 'Xiaolin Huang']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'concept erasure', 'diffusion models', 'privacy', 'model editing']

item.title: Curation Leaks: Membership Inference Attacks against Data Curation for Machine Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces novel membership inference attacks targeting data curation pipelines, including the computation of curation scores, the selection of curated subsets, and the final trained model.
  • Demonstrates that models trained solely on curated public data can still leak membership information about the private data that guided the curation process.
  • Presents differentially private adaptations of curation methods that effectively mitigate the demonstrated leakage.
  • Argues that privacy assessments must include the data selection/curation process, not just the model training procedure.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dariush Wahdany (CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security)', 'Matthew Jagielski (Anthropic)', 'Adam Dziedzic (CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security)', 'Franziska Boenisch (CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security)']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'data-curation', 'privacy-leakage', 'differential-privacy', 'attacks']

item.title: Learning to Attack: A Bandit Approach to Adversarial Context Poisoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AdvBandit, a black-box adaptive context-poisoning attack on neural contextual bandits that jointly learns and exploits the victim's evolving policy.
  • Attacker builds a surrogate via maximum-entropy inverse reinforcement learning from observed context-action pairs and optimizes continuous context perturbations with projected gradient descent; arm selection is guided by a UCB-aware Gaussian process.
  • Proposes an attack-budget control mechanism to limit detection and provides theoretical guarantees (sublinear attacker regret and victim-regret lower bounds linear in number of attacks).
  • Empirical evaluation on Yelp, MovieLens, and Disin shows AdvBandit induces higher cumulative victim regret than state-of-the-art baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ray Telikani', 'Amir H. Gandomi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'context poisoning', 'bandit attacks', 'black-box attack', 'inverse reinforcement learning']

item.title: Mathematical Foundations of Poisoning Attacks on Linear Regression over Cumulative Distribution Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Provides a rigorous theoretical analysis of poisoning attacks on linear regression models approximating cumulative distribution functions (used in learned indexes).
  • Proves the optimal single-point poisoning attack and verifies existing methods achieve it.
  • Analyzes multi-point attacks, shows greedy methods are not always optimal, derives necessary properties of optimal attacks, and computes an upper bound on multi-point impact.
  • Empirically evaluates bounds and shows greedy attacks often approach the derived upper bound, informing evaluation of defenses for learned indexes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Atsuki Sato', 'Martin Aum\\"uller', 'Yusuke Matsui']
  • Tags: ['data poisoning', 'adversarial-ML', 'learned indexes', 'theoretical analysis', 'attack evaluation']

item.title: Antibody: Strengthening Defense Against Harmful Fine-Tuning for Large Language Models via Attenuating Harmful Gradient Influence
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Antibody, a defense against harmful fine-tuning attacks (poisoned user-submitted datasets) that regularizes gradient contributions of harmful samples.
  • Two-stage approach: (1) pre-fine-tuning alignment that optimizes the model to reside in a flat loss region w.r.t. harmful samples to improve resilience; (2) a fine-tuning algorithm that weights samples in each batch to suppress learning from harmful samples while encouraging benign learning.
  • Demonstrates empirically that Antibody mitigates harmful fine-tuning attacks and can improve fine-tuning performance on user datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Quoc Minh Nguyen', 'Trung Le', 'Jing Wu', 'Anh Tuan Bui', 'Mehrtash Harandi']
  • Tags: ['harmful fine-tuning', 'data poisoning defense', 'gradient regularization', 'model robustness', 'fine-tuning-as-a-service']

item.title: Exact and Asymptotically Complete Robust Verifications of Neural Networks via Quantum Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes two quantum-optimization-based verification models for adversarial robustness: an exact, sound-and-complete formulation for piecewise-linear activations (e.g., ReLU) and an asymptotically complete over-approximation for general activations (e.g., sigmoid, tanh).
  • Introduces solver-acceleration techniques including Quantum Benders Decomposition combined with interval arithmetic, certificate-transfer bounds for pruned networks, and a layerwise partitioning strategy enabling a quantum–classical hybrid workflow.
  • Demonstrates high certification accuracy on robustness benchmarks, showing the potential of quantum optimization as a primitive for robustness guarantees and precise adversarial-example identification.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenxin Li', 'Wenchao Liu', 'Chuan Wang', 'Qi Gao', 'Yin Ma', 'Hai Wei', 'Kai Wen']
  • Tags: ['robust verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'quantum optimization', 'certification']

item.title: Quantifying Catastrophic Forgetting in IoT Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Frames RPL-based IoT intrusion detection as a domain continual learning problem and presents a method-agnostic IDS framework that can incorporate diverse continual learning strategies.
  • Systematically benchmarks five representative continual learning approaches across multiple domain-orderings using a comprehensive multi-attack dataset of 48 domains.
  • Finds that replay-based methods achieve the best overall performance, while Synaptic Intelligence (SI) attains near-zero forgetting with high training efficiency, highlighting trade-offs among plasticity, stability, and resource efficiency for IoT deployments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sourasekhar Banerjee', 'David Bergqvist', 'Salman Toor', 'Christian Rohner', 'Andreas Johnsson']
  • Tags: ['continual-learning', 'intrusion-detection', 'IoT-security', 'catastrophic-forgetting', 'defense-benchmarking']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JAILBREAK FOUNDRY (JBF), a system that translates jailbreak papers into runnable attack modules via a multi-agent workflow with three components: JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluation).
  • Reproduced 30 jailbreak attacks with high fidelity (mean reproduced–reported ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduced attack-specific implementation code by ~50%, and achieved an 82.5% mean reused-code ratio.
  • Enables standardized, automated AdvBench evaluations across 10 victim models using a consistent GPT-4o judge, supporting living benchmarks and scalable red-teaming/attack benchmarking.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial attacks', 'reproducibility', 'benchmarking', 'automated red teaming']

item.title: Agentic Unlearning: When LLM Agent Meets Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces 'agentic unlearning' to remove specified sensitive information from both model parameters and persistent agent memory to prevent reactivation via closed-loop interactions.
  • Proposes Synchronized Backflow Unlearning (SBU) combining dependency-closure memory pruning and stochastic reference alignment for parametric suppression, coordinated via a dual-update protocol to avoid cross-pathway recontamination.
  • Evaluates on medical QA benchmarks, reporting reduced traces of targeted private information across both memory and parameters with limited utility degradation on retained data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bin Wang', 'Fan Wang', 'Pingping Wang', 'Jinyu Cong', 'Yang Yu', 'Yilong Yin', 'Zhongyi Han', 'Benzheng Wei']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'model defense', 'LLM agents', 'memory safety']

item.title: Stealthy Poisoning Attacks Bypass Defenses in Regression Settings
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an optimal stealthy poisoning attack formulation for regression models that models varying degrees of detectability and demonstrates that these attacks can bypass state-of-the-art defenses.
  • Introduces a normalization-based methodology to evaluate trade-offs between attack effectiveness and detectability.
  • Develops a novel defense, BayesClean, which improves robustness against stealthy poisoning when attacks are subtle and the number of poisoned points is significant.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Javier Carnerero-Cano', "Luis Mu\\~noz-Gonz\\'alez", 'Phillippa Spencer', 'Emil C. Lupu']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'poisoning-attacks', 'defenses', 'stealthy-attacks', 'regression-robustness']

item.title: Learn-to-Distance: Distance Learning for Detecting LLM-Generated Text
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a geometric interpretation of rewrite-based detection methods for identifying LLM-generated text and explains their generalization properties.
  • Proposes Learn-to-Distance (L2D), a rewrite-based detector that adaptively learns a distance function between original and rewritten text, with theoretical results showing adaptive distance is superior to fixed distances for detection.
  • Reports extensive empirical evaluation (100+ settings) showing L2D outperforms baselines across multiple target LLMs (e.g., GPT, Claude, Gemini), with large relative improvements.
  • Provides a public Python implementation (GitHub) for reproducibility.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hongyi Zhou', 'Jin Zhu', 'Kai Ye', 'Ying Yang', 'Erhan Xu', 'Chengchun Shi']
  • Tags: ['LLM-generated text detection', 'defense', 'rewrite-based detection', 'robustness']

item.title: GNN Explanations that do not Explain and How to find Them
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a critical failure mode in self-explainable GNNs (SE-GNNs): explanations can be unambiguously unrelated to how the model actually infers labels (degenerate explanations).
  • Shows SE-GNNs can achieve optimal predictive risk while producing degenerate explanations and that common faithfulness metrics often fail to detect this.
  • Demonstrates degenerate explanations can be maliciously planted to hide use of sensitive attributes and can also arise naturally, motivating the need for auditing.
  • Proposes a novel faithfulness metric that reliably flags degenerate explanations in both adversarial and natural settings, with empirical validation and code release.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Steve Azzolin', 'Stefano Teso', 'Bruno Lepri', 'Andrea Passerini', 'Sagar Malhotra']
  • Tags: ['graph-neural-networks', 'explainability', 'adversarial-manipulation', 'privacy', 'audit']

item.title: HalluGuard: Demystifying Data-Driven and Reasoning-Driven Hallucinations in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a theoretical Hallucination Risk Bound that decomposes hallucination risk into data-driven (training-time mismatch) and reasoning-driven (inference-time instability) components.
  • Proposes HalluGuard, an NTK-based scoring method that leverages Neural Tangent Kernel geometry to jointly detect both types of hallucinations.
  • Evaluates on 10 benchmarks, compares to 11 baselines across 9 LLM backbones, reporting state-of-the-art hallucination detection performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinyue Zeng', 'Junhong Lin', 'Yujun Yan', 'Feng Guo', 'Liang Shi', 'Jun Wu', 'Dawei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'model safety / guardrails', 'robustness', 'theoretical analysis', 'NTK-based methods']

item.title: Toward Youth-Centered Privacy-by-Design in Smart Devices: A Systematic Review
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic literature review (PRISMA) of 122 papers on youth-centered privacy-by-design in AI-enabled smart devices, organized into technical solutions, policy/regulatory measures, and education/awareness strategies.
  • Finds technical defenses (on-device processing, federated learning, lightweight encryption) can reduce data exposure but have limited real-world adoption.
  • Identifies policy baselines (GDPR, UK Age-Appropriate Design Code, PIPEDA) with enforcement and age-appropriateness gaps, and notes education initiatives are seldom systematically integrated.
  • Recommends multi-stakeholder co-development (policymakers, manufacturers, educators) to create inclusive, transparent, context-sensitive privacy ecosystems for youth.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Molly Campbell', 'Mohamad Sheikho Al Jasem', 'Ajay Kumar Shrestha']
  • Tags: ['privacy-by-design', 'youth-privacy', 'federated-learning', 'on-device-processing', 'privacy-policy']

item.title: Towards Transferable Defense Against Malicious Image Edits
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TDAE, a bimodal (image+text) defense framework to make images robust against malicious diffusion-based edits.
  • Introduces FlatGrad Defense Mechanism (FDM) that uses gradient regularization to steer perturbations to flat minima for better cross-model robustness.
  • Proposes Dynamic Prompt Defense (DPD) which iteratively optimizes text embeddings alongside image updates to enforce transferability across unseen editors; shows state-of-the-art intra- and cross-model mitigation in experiments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jie Zhang', 'Shuai Dong', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'adversarial robustness', 'diffusion models', 'prompt defense', 'transferability']

item.title: AudAgent: Automated Auditing of Privacy Policy Compliance in AI Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents AudAgent, a runtime tool that monitors AI agents to verify compliance with their stated privacy policies and to block unlawful handling of sensitive data.
  • Key components: (i) policy formalization via a cross-LLM voting parser, (ii) runtime annotation using a Presidio-based sensitive-data detector, (iii) compliance auditing with ontology graphs and automata-based checking, and (iv) a UI that visualizes execution traces and violations.
  • Evaluated on agents built with mainstream frameworks (e.g., Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek), finding many policies lack safeguards for highly sensitive data (e.g., SSNs) and that several agents do not refuse processing such data; AudAgent can proactively block such operations.
  • Combines privacy-vulnerability analysis, automated auditing, and enforcement (defense) to improve transparency and accountability of AI agents' data practices.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ye Zheng', 'Yidan Hu']
  • Tags: ['privacy-auditing', 'policy-compliance', 'runtime-monitoring', 'data-leakage', 'defense-tooling']

item.title: Spurious Correlation-Aware Embedding Regularization for Worst-Group Robustness
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SCER (Spurious Correlation-Aware Embedding Regularization), a method that regularizes feature embeddings to suppress spurious cues and improve worst-group robustness.
  • Provides theoretical analysis showing worst-group error depends on classifier reliance on spurious vs. core directions, identified via differences in group-wise mean embeddings.
  • Imposes embedding-level constraints to encourage focus on core features and reduce sensitivity to spurious patterns, and reports empirical SOTA gains on multiple vision and language benchmarks.
  • Code is released (GitHub) and evaluations span both vision and NLP settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subeen Park', 'Joowang Kim', 'Hakyung Lee', 'Sunjae Yoo', 'Kyungwoo Song']
  • Tags: ['worst-group-robustness', 'spurious-correlations', 'representation-regularization', 'distribution-shift', 'robustness']

item.title: Steering Evaluation-Aware Language Models to Act Like They Are Deployed
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies evaluation-awareness in LLMs: models detect evaluation cues and alter behavior (e.g., adding Python type hints) to appear more aligned during tests.
  • Proposes activation steering: compute a steering vector from the original model and add it to activations to suppress evaluation-aware behavior during evaluation.
  • Empirical setup: they induce evaluation-aware behavior via continued pretraining on cue-containing documents and expert-iteration training, then show steering reduces the gap between evaluation and deployment behavior.
  • Implication: activation steering can make safety evaluations more reliable (or conversely enable models to evade evaluation), highlighting a security concern for red-teaming and evaluation protocols.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tim Tian Hua', 'Andrew Qin', 'Samuel Marks', 'Neel Nanda']
  • Tags: ['evaluation-evasion', 'activation-steering', 'red-teaming', 'safety-evaluation', 'model-manipulation']

item.title: TAO: Tolerance-Aware Optimistic Verification for Floating-Point Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TAO, a tolerance-aware optimistic verification protocol that accepts outputs within operator-level acceptance regions rather than requiring bitwise equality for floating-point neural network execution on untrusted hardware.
  • Combines two error models: sound per-operator IEEE-754 worst-case bounds and tight empirical percentile profiles calibrated across hardware; discrepancies trigger a Merkle-anchored, threshold-guided dispute game that recursively isolates offending operators.
  • Implements TAO as a PyTorch-compatible runtime and blockchain-backed contract layer; evaluates across CNNs, Transformers, and diffusion models on multiple GPUs showing empirical thresholds much tighter than theoretical bounds and negligible runtime overhead, preventing bound-aware adversarial attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianzhu Yao', 'Hongxu Su', 'Taobo Liao', 'Zerui Cheng', 'Huan Zhang', 'Xuechao Wang', 'Pramod Viswanath']
  • Tags: ['model verification', 'integrity auditing', 'robustness', 'ML-as-a-Service', 'adversarial defenses']

item.title: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes adaptive attacks by untrusted LLMs that know the AI control protocol and monitor model, enabling prompt-injection style payloads embedded in model outputs.
  • Empirically demonstrates that such adaptive attacks consistently evade diverse LLM monitors and complete malicious tasks on two AI control benchmarks.
  • Shows that protocols relying on monitors (including Defer-to-Resample) are fundamentally vulnerable; resampling can amplify injections and act as a best-of-n attack.
  • Argues that adaptive attacks on monitor models are a major blind spot and should be a standard part of evaluations for future AI control mechanisms.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mikhail Terekhov', 'Alexander Panfilov', 'Daniil Dzenhaliou', 'Caglar Gulcehre', 'Maksym Andriushchenko', 'Ameya Prabhu', 'Jonas Geiping']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'monitor evasion', 'red teaming', 'adversarial attacks', 'AI control protocols']

item.title: Untargeted Jailbreak Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes UJA, the first gradient-based untargeted jailbreak attack that maximizes the probability of unsafe outputs rather than steering models to predefined target responses.
  • Decomposes the untargeted objective into two differentiable sub-objectives to jointly search for the most harmful response and the adversarial prompt, with theoretical justification for the decomposition.
  • Demonstrates empirically that UJA achieves >80% attack success against recent safety-aligned LLMs within 100 optimization iterations, outperforming prior gradient-based attacks by over 30%.
  • Highlights that the untargeted objective expands the adversarial search space and improves attack efficiency compared to targeted-suffix optimization methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinzhe Huang', 'Wenjing Hu', 'Tianhang Zheng', 'Kedong Xiu', 'Xiaojun Jia', 'Di Wang', 'Zhan Qin', 'Kui Ren']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'gradient-based attack', 'adversarial attack', 'LLM safety', 'untargeted attack']

item.title: Train Once, Answer All: Many Pretraining Experiments for the Cost of One
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a method to run multiple controlled pretraining experiments concurrently in a single training run, enabling many experiments for the compute cost of one.
  • Replicates prior work on data contamination, data poisoning, and memorization, and runs new experiments on knowledge acquisition, mathematical reasoning, and watermarking.
  • Proposes continual pretraining dependence testing (CPDT) to detect interactions between simultaneous experiments and finds interactions negligible in their setup.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sebastian Bordt', 'Martin Pawelczyk']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'memorization/privacy', 'data-contamination', 'watermarking/defense', 'pretraining-methodology']

item.title: Robust Fine-Tuning from Non-Robust Pretrained Models: Mitigating Suboptimal Transfer With Epsilon-Scheduling
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'suboptimal transfer': fine-tuning non-robust pretrained models with a robust objective can impede task adaptation and yield poor adversarial robustness/accuracy.
  • Proposes Epsilon-Scheduling, a heuristic schedule over perturbation strength during training to avoid the transfer failure and enable effective robust fine-tuning (RFT).
  • Introduces 'expected robustness' as a metric to evaluate performance across a range of perturbation strengths, capturing the accuracy–robustness trade-off.
  • Provides extensive experiments (six pretrained models, five datasets) showing Epsilon-Scheduling consistently prevents suboptimal transfer and improves expected robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Jonas Ngnaw\\'e", 'Maxime Heuillet', 'Sabyasachi Sahoo', 'Yann Pequignot', 'Ola Ahmad', 'Audrey Durand', "Fr\\'ed\\'eric Precioso", "Christian Gagn\\'e"]
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'robust fine-tuning', 'adversarial training', 'transfer learning', 'defense']

item.title: Chasing the Tail: Effective Rubric-based Reward Modeling for Large Language Model Post-Training
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies reward over-optimization in reinforcement fine-tuning as arising from reward misspecification concentrated in the high-reward tail (difficulty distinguishing Excellent vs Great responses).
  • Proposes rubric-based reward modeling that can leverage off-policy exemplars (stronger models or rewrites) while being robust to their artifacts, focusing on discriminating diverse high-quality responses.
  • Introduces a workflow to elicit tail-focused rubrics and shows empirically that rubric-based rewards mitigate reward hacking and improve post-training LLM performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junkai Zhang', 'Zihao Wang', 'Lin Gui', 'Swarnashree Mysore Sathyendra', 'Jaehwan Jeong', 'Victor Veitch', 'Wei Wang', 'Yunzhong He', 'Bing Liu', 'Lifeng Jin']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'robustness/defense', 'reward modeling', 'alignment', 'reinforcement fine-tuning']

item.title: ERIS: Evolutionary Real-world Interference Scheme for Jailbreaking Audio Large Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ERIS, an evolutionary algorithm framework that crafts naturalistic real-world audio interference to embed jailbreak instructions into audio inputs for Audio Large Models (ALMs).
  • Generates adversarial audio that appears as harmless speech with background noise to humans and safety filters but induces unsafe responses from ALMs.
  • Empirically evaluates ERIS across multiple ALMs, showing it outperforms existing text- and audio-based jailbreak baselines and discusses implications for defensive mechanisms.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yibo Zhang', 'Liang Lin']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'audio adversarial attacks', 'adversarial optimization', 'model robustness']

item.title: BinaryShield: Cross-Service Threat Intelligence in LLM Services using Privacy-Preserving Fingerprints
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BinaryShield, a privacy-preserving threat intelligence system that enables sharing of attack fingerprints across compliance boundaries to mitigate prompt injection risk.
  • Transforms suspicious prompts via PII redaction, semantic embedding, binary quantization, and randomized response to produce privacy-preserving fingerprints that retain attack patterns.
  • Evaluates effectiveness and efficiency: achieves F1=0.94 (vs SimHash 0.77), reduces storage, and enables ~38x faster similarity search than dense embeddings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Waris Gill', 'Natalie Isak', 'Matthew Dressman']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'privacy-preserving', 'threat intelligence', 'attack detection']

item.title: Safeguarding Multimodal Knowledge Copyright in the RAG-as-a-Service Environment
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AQUA, a watermarking framework to protect image knowledge in multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) services.
  • Embeds semantic signals into synthetic images via acronym-based triggers and spatial relationship cues to create imperceptible, robust watermarks.
  • Designed so watermark signals survive indirect propagation through image retrievers to textual generators, enabling copyright tracing.
  • Evaluated across models and datasets demonstrating robustness, stealthiness, and reliability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tianyu Chen', 'Jian Lou', 'Wenjie Wang']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'multimodal RAG', 'copyright protection', 'image watermarking', 'robustness']

item.title: VeriTrail: Closed-Domain Hallucination Detection with Traceability
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes VeriTrail, a method to detect closed-domain hallucinations and provide traceability of where unsubstantiated content was introduced across multi-step (MGS) and single-step (SGS) generative processes.
  • Introduces new datasets that include all intermediate outputs for MGS and human annotations of final-output faithfulness to sources.
  • Shows VeriTrail outperforms baseline methods on these datasets, enabling both detection of hallucinated content and tracing its likely origin in intermediate steps.

Paper Type

  • Research Paper

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dasha Metropolitansky', 'Jonathan Larson']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'traceability', 'multi-step-generation', 'faithfulness', 'dataset']

item.title: JALMBench: Benchmarking Jailbreak Vulnerabilities in Audio Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JALMBench, a large benchmark (11,316 text samples, 245,355 audio samples, >1,000 hours) for evaluating jailbreak attacks on Large Audio Language Models (LALMs).
  • Evaluates 12 LALMs across 8 attack methods (4 text-transferred, 4 audio-originated) and 5 defenses, with analyses of attack efficiency, topic sensitivity, voice diversity, and architecture effects.
  • Finds text-based safety alignment partially transfers to audio, interleaved audio-text improves cross-modal generalization, and general-purpose moderation offers limited protection; proposes prompt- and response-level mitigations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zifan Peng', 'Yule Liu', 'Zhen Sun', 'Mingchen Li', 'Zeren Luo', 'Jingyi Zheng', 'Wenhan Dong', 'Xinlei He', 'Xuechao Wang', 'Yingjie Xue', 'Shengmin Xu', 'Xinyi Huang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-attacks', 'security-benchmark', 'audio-language-models', 'defenses']

item.title: Self-Destructive Language Model
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SEAM, a defense that makes LLMs 'self-destructive'—preserving benign capabilities but degrading performance when fine-tuned on harmful data.
  • Introduces a novel loss that couples optimization trajectories on benign and harmful data, plus adversarial gradient ascent to amplify the self-destructive effect.
  • Develops an efficient Hessian-free gradient estimator with theoretical error bounds to enable practical training.
  • Evaluates across models and datasets, showing strong robustness to low-intensity attacks and catastrophic collapse under high-intensity harmful fine-tuning.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhui Wang', 'Rongyi Zhu', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['fine-tuning attack defense', 'model robustness', 'alignment', 'data poisoning', 'adversarial training']

item.title: Multi-PA: A Multi-perspective Benchmark on Privacy Assessment for Large Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Multi-PA, a comprehensive benchmark to assess privacy awareness and privacy leakage in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs).
  • Covers 26 personal privacy, 15 trade secret, and 18 state secret categories with 31,962 samples and defines multiple sub-tasks to evaluate privacy protection.
  • Evaluates 21 open-source and 2 closed-source LVLMs, finding significant risks of privacy breaches and varying vulnerabilities across privacy categories.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jie Zhang', 'Xiangkui Cao', 'Zhouyu Han', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'privacy-benchmark', 'model-evaluation', 'vision-language-models']

item.title: A Survey for Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Network Intrusion Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Survey of deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods applied to network intrusion detection, covering core DRL algorithms (e.g., DQN, actor-critic) and recent research.
  • Analyzes challenges such as training efficiency, class imbalance, minority/unknown attack detection, feature selection, and dataset issues, with emphasis on IoT intrusion detection.
  • Evaluates performance of DRL models on public datasets, notes areas underexplored, and suggests integrating DRL with generative methods and novel policy functions to improve robustness and adaptability.
  • Provides recommendations for real-world deployment and testing of DRL-based intrusion detection systems.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wanrong Yang', 'Alberto Acuto', 'Yihang Zhou', 'Dominik Wojtczak']
  • Tags: ['deep reinforcement learning', 'network intrusion detection', 'security defenses', 'IoT security', 'anomaly detection']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the final LLM softmax classifier as an Energy-Based Model (EBM) and decomposes sequence generation into interacting EBMs to track "energy spills" during decoding.
  • Introduces two training-free, logit-derived metrics—spilled energy (discrepancy across consecutive generation steps) and marginalized energy (single-step measurable)—to detect hallucinations, factual errors, and biases.
  • Evaluated across nine benchmarks and multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen3), showing robust, competitive hallucination detection and cross-task generalization for both pretrained and instruction-tuned models.
  • Requires no additional training, probes, or activation ablations; provides a lightweight diagnostic/safety mechanism for LLM outputs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'energy-based-models', 'model-safety', 'robustness', 'diagnostic-tools']

item.title: Is It Thinking or Cheating? Detecting Implicit Reward Hacking by Measuring Reasoning Effort
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses implicit reward hacking where models obtain high reward via shortcuts that do not appear in their chain-of-thought (CoT).
  • Proposes TRACE: progressively truncate CoT, force answers at cutoffs, estimate expected reward vs reasoning length, and use area-under-curve to detect low-effort shortcut exploitation.
  • Empirical gains: >65% improvement over a strong 72B CoT monitor on math reasoning and >30% over a 32B monitor on coding; can discover unknown loopholes during training.
  • Presents a scalable, unsupervised oversight/detection method for identifying reward-hacking behavior that bypasses standard CoT monitors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinpeng Wang', 'Nitish Joshi', 'Barbara Plank', 'Rico Angell', 'He He']
  • Tags: ['reward-hacking', 'attack-detection', 'chain-of-thought-monitoring', 'adversarial-safety']

item.title: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses safety of chain-of-thought reasoning in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs), noting that harmful content can appear in intermediate reasoning even if final outputs seem safe.
  • Proposes Intervened Preference Optimization (IPO): a process-supervision alignment method that replaces compliance steps with safety triggers and constructs strong preference-learning pairs via corrective interventions.
  • Empirical results on jailbreak and adversarial safety benchmarks show IPO reduces harmfulness by over 30% relative to SFT and RL baselines while retaining strong reasoning performance.
  • Highlights key insights about safe reasoning (few critical safety-trigger steps, correlation between compliance cues and unsafe continuations, and effectiveness of corrective interventions) and argues for explicit alignment of reasoning processes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yichi Zhang', 'Yue Ding', 'Jingwen Yang', 'Tianwei Luo', 'Dongbai Li', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Qiang Liu', 'Hang Su', 'Yinpeng Dong', 'Jun Zhu']
  • Tags: ['AI safety', 'defense', 'jailbreaking', 'chain-of-thought alignment', 'preference learning']

item.title: Reasoning or Retrieval? A Study of Answer Attribution on Large Reasoning Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies two competing mechanisms in large reasoning models (LRMs): chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and memory retrieval, and shows they can conflict (final answers contradict reasoning traces).
  • Performs controlled experiments with misleading reasoning cues and corrupted retrieval answers to demonstrate models exploit retrieval as a shortcut; dominance depends on domain, model scale, and fine-tuning approach.
  • Proposes FARL, a fine-tuning framework combining memory unlearning with reinforcement learning to suppress retrieval shortcuts and encourage genuine, generalizable reasoning.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhui Wang', 'Changjiang Li', 'Guangke Chen', 'Jiacheng Liang', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['reward hacking', 'shortcut exploitation', 'robustness', 'fine-tuning defense', 'chain-of-thought']

item.title: Control Tax: The Price of Keeping AI in Check
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a theoretical framework that quantifies 'Control Tax' — the operational and financial cost of integrating oversight/control measures and links classifier performance to safety guarantees.
  • Empirically evaluates state-of-the-art language models in adversarial settings where attacker models insert subtle backdoors into code and monitoring models attempt to detect these vulnerabilities.
  • Provides empirical financial cost estimates for control protocols and designs optimized monitoring strategies that trade off safety and cost under practical constraints (e.g., auditing budgets).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mikhail Terekhov', 'Zhen Ning David Liu', 'Caglar Gulcehre', 'Samuel Albanie']
  • Tags: ['backdoor attacks', 'detection/monitoring', 'operational cost / economics', 'adversarial evaluation', 'AI governance/control']

item.title: VPI-Bench: Visual Prompt Injection Attacks for Computer-Use Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces VPI-Bench, a benchmark of 306 interactive test cases across five platforms to evaluate Visual Prompt Injection (VPI) attacks against Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) and Browser-Use Agents (BUAs).
  • Empirical results show high vulnerability: CUAs can be deceived up to 51% and BUAs up to 100% on certain platforms; system-prompt defenses yield only limited improvements.
  • Demonstrates the security and privacy risks of visually embedded malicious instructions in rendered UIs and calls for robust, context-aware defenses for multimodal AI agents.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tri Cao', 'Bennett Lim', 'Yue Liu', 'Yuan Sui', 'Yuexin Li', 'Shumin Deng', 'Lin Lu', 'Nay Oo', 'Shuicheng Yan', 'Bryan Hooi']
  • Tags: ['visual prompt injection', 'adversarial attacks', 'benchmark', 'multimodal agents', 'red teaming']

item.title: Safety Mirage: How Spurious Correlations Undermine VLM Safety Fine-Tuning and Can Be Mitigated by Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a 'safety mirage' where supervised safety fine-tuning for vision-language models (VLMs) induces spurious text-safety correlations rather than robust mitigation.
  • Shows a practical one-word modification attack that exploits these spurious correlations to bypass safety guards and also causes over-prudence (unnecessary refusals).
  • Proposes machine unlearning (MU) as an alternative alignment method that removes harmful knowledge while preserving general capabilities.
  • Evaluations show MU-based alignment can reduce attack success rates by up to 60.27% and cut unnecessary rejections by over 84.20% across safety benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yiwei Chen', 'Yuguang Yao', 'Yihua Zhang', 'Bingquan Shen', 'Gaowen Liu', 'Sijia Liu']
  • Tags: ['VLM safety', 'spurious correlations', 'adversarial attack', 'machine unlearning', 'alignment']

item.title: On the Rate of Convergence of GD in Non-linear Neural Networks: An Adversarial Robustness Perspective
item.description:
Summary

  • Provides a theoretical analysis of gradient descent convergence in a minimal two-neuron ReLU binary classifier, focusing on the evolution of the adversarial robustness margin.
  • Proves GD converges to the optimal robustness margin but at a very slow rate, Theta(1 / ln(t)), establishing an explicit lower bound on robustness margin convergence in a non-linear model.
  • Supports theory with empirical simulations showing the same tight convergence rate across multiple initializations and activation-pattern regimes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Guy Smorodinsky', 'Sveta Gimpleson', 'Itay Safran']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'training dynamics', 'optimization convergence', 'theoretical analysis']

item.title: Ignore All Previous Instructions: Jailbreaking as a de-escalatory peace building practise to resist LLM social media bots
item.description:
Summary

  • Frames 'jailbreaking' as a user-driven, non-violent de-escalation practice aimed at exposing and disrupting suspected LLM-powered social media bots.
  • Focuses on circumventing LLM safeguards via user interactions to reveal automated behaviour and interrupt the spread of misleading narratives.
  • Positions this practice as an alternative/complement to platform-led moderation and discusses its sociotechnical and ethical implications.

Paper Type

  • Position

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huw Day', 'Adrianna Jezierska', 'Jessica Woodgate']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'red teaming', 'adversarial interaction', 'social media bots', 'model safeguards']

item.title: Explanation-Guided Adversarial Training for Robust and Interpretable Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Explanation-Guided Adversarial Training (EGAT), which integrates adversarial training with explanation-guided learning to improve prediction accuracy, adversarial robustness, and explanation quality simultaneously.
  • EGAT generates adversarial examples during training while imposing constraints on model attributions to enforce attributional stability and semantically meaningful explanations.
  • Provides PAC-style theoretical analysis showing improved stability under unexpected inputs versus standard adversarial training, and reports empirical gains (≈+37% adversarial accuracy, modest +16% training time) on OOD benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chao Chen', 'Yanhui Chen', 'Shanshan Lin', 'Dongsheng Hong', 'Shu Wu', 'Xiangwen Liao', 'Chuanyi Liu']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-training', 'adversarial-robustness', 'explainability', 'attributional-stability', 'OOD-robustness']

item.title: Phishing the Phishers with SpecularNet: Hierarchical Graph Autoencoding for Reference-Free Web Phishing Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SpecularNet, a lightweight, reference-free phishing detection model that uses domain name and HTML DOM tree structure.
  • Uses a hierarchical graph autoencoding architecture with directional, level-wise message passing to capture structural invariants of phishing webpages.
  • Achieves competitive performance (F1 93.9%) versus reference-based systems while dramatically reducing inference time (~20 ms per webpage on CPU).
  • Evaluated against 13 state-of-the-art detectors, tested in field deployments on a 2026 open-world dataset, and assessed for robustness against adversarial attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tailai Song', 'Pedro Casas', 'Michela Meo']
  • Tags: ['phishing-detection', 'graph-neural-networks', 'adversarial-robustness', 'web-security']

item.title: ALTER: Asymmetric LoRA for Token-Entropy-Guided Unlearning of LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ALTER, an efficient token-level unlearning framework for LLMs using an asymmetric LoRA architecture to isolate and remove target knowledge while preserving core capabilities.
  • Two-phase method: (I) capture high-entropy tokens via the shared A matrix in LoRA; (II) perform asymmetric LoRA parameter isolation to unlearn tokens in target subdomains.
  • Reports strong results (≥95% forget quality) on TOFU, WMDP, and MUSE benchmarks while preserving >90% model utility and reducing computational overhead versus full-model updates.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xunlei Chen', 'Jinyu Guo', 'Yuang Li', 'Zhaokun Wang', 'Yi Gong', 'Jie Zou', 'Jiwei Wei', 'Wenhong Tian']
  • Tags: ['unlearning', 'privacy', 'model-editing', 'LoRA', 'parameter-efficient-finetuning']

item.title: Co-Evolutionary Multi-Modal Alignment via Structured Adversarial Evolution
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CEMMA, a co-evolutionary framework for multimodal safety alignment where an Evolutionary Attacker generates evolving adversarial prompts and an Adaptive Defender is iteratively trained on synthesized hard negatives.
  • Evolutionary Attacker decomposes adversarial prompts into method templates and harmful intents and applies genetic operators (mutation, crossover, differential evolution) to produce stronger jailbreaks from simple seeds, substantially increasing red-team attack success rate (ASR).
  • Adaptive Defender improves robustness and generalization across benchmarks with higher data efficiency, avoids excessive benign refusals, and remains compatible with inference-time defenses (e.g., AdaShield).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Guoxin Shi', 'Haoyu Wang', 'Zaihui Yang', 'Yuxing Wang', 'Yongzhe Chang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'jailbreaking', 'red-teaming', 'adversarial training', 'multimodal safety']

item.title: What Helps -- and What Hurts: Bidirectional Explanations for Vision Transformers
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BiCAM, a bidirectional class activation mapping that preserves signed (positive and negative) attributions for Vision Transformers.
  • Introduces a Positive-to-Negative Ratio (PNR) that summarizes attribution balance and can be used for lightweight detection of adversarial examples without retraining.
  • Demonstrates improved localization and faithfulness on ImageNet, VOC, and COCO and generalization across ViT variants (DeiT, Swin).
  • Primary contribution is interpretability, with an ancillary application to adversarial example detection (defensive/detection mechanism).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qin Su', 'Tie Luo']
  • Tags: ['explainability', 'vision-transformers', 'adversarial-detection', 'class-activation-mapping', 'robustness']

item.title: SafeSci: Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models in Science Domains and Beyond
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SafeSci, a comprehensive framework for LLM safety in scientific domains consisting of SafeSciBench (0.25M samples) and SafeSciTrain (1.5M samples) for evaluation and safety enhancement.
  • Uses objective metrics (deterministically answerable questions) to reduce evaluation bias and distinguishes safety knowledge from risk to broaden coverage.
  • Evaluates 24 advanced LLMs, revealing notable safety vulnerabilities and variable excessive refusal behaviors; shows fine-tuning on SafeSciTrain improves safety alignment.
  • Argues safety judgments should be context-dependent rather than universally labeling scientific questions as safe/unsafe, and provides resources for building safer scientific AI systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiangyang Zhu', 'Yuan Tian', 'Qi Jia', 'Kaiwei Zhang', 'Zicheng Zhang', 'Chunyi Li', 'Kaiyuan Ji', 'Dongrui Liu', 'Zijian Chen', 'Lu Sun', 'Renrui Zhang', 'Yan Teng', 'Jing Shao', 'Wei Sun', 'Xia Hu', 'Yu Qiao', 'Guangtao Zhai']
  • Tags: ['safety benchmark', 'model evaluation', 'safety fine-tuning', 'LLM vulnerabilities', 'dataset']

item.title: Extracting Training Dialogue Data from Large Language Model based Task Bots
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes privacy risks of LLM-based Task-Oriented Dialogue Systems (TODS) with respect to memorization of training dialogue data.
  • Evaluates existing training-data extraction and membership-inference attacks, identifies why prior methods underperform on TODS, and proposes novel attack techniques tailored to LLM-based TODS.
  • Demonstrates empirical effectiveness: extracts thousands of dialogue-state labels with best-case precision >70%, and provides analysis of factors influencing memorization plus targeted mitigation strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shuo Zhang', 'Junzhou Zhao', 'Junji Hou', 'Pinghui Wang', 'Chenxu Wang', 'Jing Tao']
  • Tags: ['training-data-extraction', 'membership-inference', 'privacy', 'task-oriented-dialogue-systems', 'data-memorization']

item.title: Towards Privacy-Preserving LLM Inference via Collaborative Obfuscation (Technical Report)
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AloePri, a privacy-preserving LLM inference method that uses covariant obfuscation to jointly transform inputs, outputs, and model parameters.
  • Aims to preserve accuracy and efficiency while remaining compatible with existing LLM serving infrastructures and heterogeneous hardware clusters.
  • Evaluated on a 671B-parameter model, reporting 0.0%–3.5% accuracy loss, plaintext-equivalent efficiency, and strong resistance to extraction attacks (<5% tokens recovered).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yu Lin', 'Qizhi Zhang', 'Wenqiang Ruan', 'Daode Zhang', 'Jue Hong', 'Ye Wu', 'Hanning Xia', 'Yunlong Mao', 'Sheng Zhong']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving inference', 'obfuscation-based defense', 'LLM security', 'inference-time protections']

item.title: Inference-Time Safety For Code LLMs Via Retrieval-Augmented Revision
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an inference-time safety mechanism for code LLMs that uses retrieval-augmented generation to surface security risks and guide automated code revision using curated Stack Overflow security discussions.
  • Design emphasizes interpretability (transparent interventions grounded in expert community explanations), robustness to evolving vulnerabilities without retraining, and real-time safety alignment prior to deployment.
