  • Empirical evaluation shows improved security of LLM-generated code vs. prompting alone and no introduction of new vulnerabilities per static analysis across benchmarks and real-world datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Manisha Mukherjee', 'Vincent J. Hellendoorn']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'safe-code-generation', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'inference-time-mitigation', 'security-evaluation']

item.title: A SUPERB-Style Benchmark of Self-Supervised Speech Models for Audio Deepfake Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Spoof-SUPERB, a SUPERB-style benchmark evaluating 20 self-supervised speech (SSL) models for audio deepfake (spoof) detection across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.
  • Finds large-scale discriminative SSL models (e.g., XLS-R, UniSpeech-SAT, WavLM Large) consistently outperform generative and spectrogram-based models, attributed to multilingual pretraining, speaker-aware objectives, and model scale.
  • Analyzes robustness under acoustic degradations, showing generative approaches degrade sharply while discriminative models remain more resilient, and provides a reproducible baseline for securing speech systems against deepfakes.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hashim Ali', 'Nithin Sai Adupa', 'Surya Subramani', 'Hafiz Malik']
  • Tags: ['audio-deepfake-detection', 'spoofing', 'benchmark', 'self-supervised-learning', 'robustness']

item.title: VidDoS: Universal Denial-of-Service Attack on Video-based Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces VidDoS, a universal Energy-Latency Attack (ELA) that crafts instance-agnostic triggers to induce denial-of-service on Video-LLMs without per-instance gradients.
  • Key techniques: masked teacher forcing to steer outputs toward long/expensive target sequences, a refusal penalty, and early-termination suppression to overcome conciseness priors.
  • Empirical results across three Video-LLMs and datasets show dramatic effects (≈205× token expansion, >15× inference latency) and demonstrate safety-critical failures in simulated real-time autonomous driving streams.
  • Focuses on attack development and evaluation, highlighting a novel high-hazard vulnerability unique to video-based multimodal LLMs and urging mitigation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Duoxun Tang', 'Dasen Dai', 'Jiyao Wang', 'Xiao Yang', 'Jianyu Wang', 'Siqi Cai']
  • Tags: ['denial-of-service', 'energy-latency attack', 'universal trigger', 'video-LLM vulnerability', 'robustness/security']

item.title: Integrating LTL Constraints into PPO for Safe Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PPO-LTL: integrates Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) safety constraints into Proximal Policy Optimization for safe reinforcement learning.
  • Uses limit-deterministic Büchi automata to monitor LTL violations, converts violations to penalty costs, and incorporates them via a Lagrangian optimization scheme.
  • Evaluated on Zones and CARLA driving environments, showing reduced safety violations while maintaining competitive task performance; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Maifang Zhang', 'Hang Yu', 'Qian Zuo', 'Cheng Wang', 'Vaishak Belle', 'Fengxiang He']
  • Tags: ['safe reinforcement learning', 'safety constraints', 'Linear Temporal Logic (LTL)', 'PPO', 'formal methods']

item.title: Attention Smoothing Is All You Need For Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Attention Smoothing Unlearning (ASU), which performs unlearning via self-distillation from a forget-teacher by increasing attention softmax temperature to flatten attention distributions.
  • Claims ASU suppresses lexical- and semantic-level associations that enable memorization, producing bounded optimization that erases factual information while preserving coherence.
  • Evaluates on unlearning benchmarks (TOFU, MUSE, WMDP) and real-world/continual unlearning scenarios across question answering and text completion, showing improved forgetting with minimal utility loss.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Saleh Zare Zade', 'Xiangyu Zhou', 'Sijia Liu', 'Dongxiao Zhu']
  • Tags: ['unlearning', 'privacy', 'model editing', 'attention-based defenses', 'self-distillation']

item.title: Token-level Data Selection for Safe LLM Fine-tuning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies safety degradation during LLM fine-tuning and diagnoses it at token-level rather than sample-level.
  • Proposes TOSS, a token-level data selection framework that scores tokens by loss difference between a safety-degraded model and a utility-oriented model to remove unsafe tokens while preserving task-relevant content.
  • Introduces TOSS-Pro, a progressive refinement that iteratively improves the safety-degraded model for better unsafe-token identification; shows improved safety-utility trade-offs versus sample-level defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanping Li', 'Zhening Liu', 'Zijian Li', 'Zehong Lin', 'Jun Zhang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'data-sanitization', 'fine-tuning', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Hide&Seek: Remove Image Watermarks with Negligible Cost via Pixel-wise Reconstruction
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes HIDE&SEEK (HS), a suite of cost-effective attacks that remove embedded watermarks from images via pixel-wise reconstruction.
  • Claims reliable watermark removal while preserving high visual fidelity and imposing negligible computational cost.
  • Evaluates weaknesses of state-of-the-art proactive image watermarking defenses, exposing robustness limits.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huajie Chen', 'Tianqing Zhu', 'Hailin Yang', 'Yuchen Zhong', 'Yang Zhang', 'Hui Sun', 'Heng Xu', 'Zuobin Ying', 'Lihua Yin', 'Wanlei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['watermark removal', 'image watermarking', 'adversarial attack', 'robustness evaluation', 'image forensics']

item.title: Turning Black Box into White Box: Dataset Distillation Leaks
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that dataset distillation can leak sensitive information because synthetic datasets encode model weight trajectories and become over-informative.
  • Introduces the Information Revelation Attack (IRA) that infers the distillation algorithm and model architecture, performs membership inference, and recovers sensitive training samples.
  • Empirical results demonstrate successful privacy attacks against state-of-the-art distillation methods, exposing significant privacy vulnerabilities in distilled datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huajie Chen', 'Tianqing Zhu', 'Yuchen Zhong', 'Yang Zhang', 'Shang Wang', 'Feng He', 'Lefeng Zhang', 'Jialiang Shen', 'Minghao Wang', 'Wanlei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['dataset-distillation', 'privacy-leakage', 'membership-inference', 'attack', 'model-inference']

item.title: From Intuition to Investigation: A Tool-Augmented Reasoning MLLM Framework for Generalizable Face Anti-Spoofing
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TAR-FAS, a tool-augmented MLLM framework that frames face anti-spoofing (FAS) as a Chain-of-Thought with Visual Tools (CoT-VT) to combine intuitive textual observations with adaptive invocation of external visual analysis tools for fine-grained spoof clue investigation.
  • Introduces a tool-augmented annotation pipeline and releases the ToolFAS-16K dataset containing multi-turn tool-use reasoning trajectories to train and evaluate tool-aware reasoning for FAS.
  • Presents a training method, Diverse-Tool Group Relative Policy Optimization (DT-GRPO), enabling models to autonomously learn efficient tool usage policies.
  • Reports state-of-the-art cross-domain performance under a one-to-eleven protocol and emphasizes improved trustworthy, fine-grained detection of presentation attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haoyuan Zhang', 'Keyao Wang', 'Guosheng Zhang', 'Haixiao Yue', 'Zhiwen Tan', 'Siran Peng', 'Tianshuo Zhang', 'Xiao Tan', 'Kunbin Chen', 'Wei He', 'Jingdong Wang', 'Ajian Liu', 'Xiangyu Zhu', 'Zhen Lei']
  • Tags: ['face-anti-spoofing', 'presentation-attack-detection', 'tool-augmented-MLLM', 'ToolFAS-16K-dataset', 'cross-domain-robustness']

item.title: AWE: Adaptive Agents for Dynamic Web Penetration Testing
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents AWE, a memory-augmented multi-agent framework that orchestrates LLMs within vulnerability-specific pipelines for autonomous web penetration testing.
  • Focuses on exploitation-driven, deterministic testing (browser-backed verification, payload mutation) targeting injection-class vulnerabilities (XSS, blind SQLi).
  • Evaluated on the XBOW benchmark, showing large gains in XSS and blind SQL injection success rates and improved efficiency vs a general-purpose LLM agent (MAPTA).
  • Provides source code and argues architectural design (specialized pipelines + persistent memory) is key to improved security testing performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Akshat Singh Jaswal', 'Ashish Baghel']
  • Tags: ['web-penetration-testing', 'adversarial-security', 'LLM-augmented-attacks', 'vulnerability-assessment', 'autonomous-agents']

item.title: AMDS: Attack-Aware Multi-Stage Defense System for Network Intrusion Detection with Two-Stage Adaptive Weight Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an attack-aware multi-stage defense for ML-based network intrusion detection that combines ensemble disagreement, predictive uncertainty, and distributional anomaly signals via learned adaptive weights.
  • Introduces a two-stage adaptive weight learning mechanism to tailor detection strategies to distinct adversarial attack signatures identified across seven attack types.
  • Empirical results show high ROC AUC (94.2%), improved accuracy and F1 over adversarially trained ensembles, and resilience to a limited set of adaptive white-box attacks; cross-dataset tests indicate dependency on baseline classifier competence and feature dimensionality.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Oluseyi Olukola', 'Nick Rahimi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial defenses', 'network intrusion detection', 'ensemble methods', 'adaptive attacks', 'anomaly detection']

item.title: Constitutional Black-Box Monitoring for Scheming in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes constitutional black-box monitors: prompted LLM classifiers that detect 'scheming' by agents using only external inputs/outputs and synthetic training data.
  • Introduces two synthetic trajectory generation pipelines (STRIDE and Gloom) to produce training datasets and optimizes monitors via prompt sweeps, human refinement, and automated prompt optimization.
  • Evaluates monitors on 7,500 held-out trajectories from ControlArena (grounded agent environments) and finds monitors trained on synthetic data can generalize to more realistic settings.
  • Finds detection performance saturates quickly: simple prompt sweeps match extensive optimization, and further tuning causes overfitting rather than improved robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simon Storf', 'Rich Barton-Cooper', 'James Peters-Gill', 'Marius Hobbhahn']
  • Tags: ['Agent monitoring', 'LLM-based defenses', 'Synthetic datasets', 'Scheming detection']

item.title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of LLM Unlearning Robustness under Multi-Turn Interaction
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates robustness of machine unlearning for LLMs in multi-turn interactive settings, beyond standard static, single-turn evaluations.
  • Evaluates two interaction patterns—self-correction and dialogue-conditioned querying—and shows that ostensibly forgotten knowledge can often be recovered through interaction.
  • Finds that stronger unlearning can produce behavioral rigidity rather than genuine knowledge erasure, and that static evaluations may overestimate real-world forgetting effectiveness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruihao Pan', 'Suhang Wang']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'robustness', 'interactive attacks', 'data removal']

item.title: ContextCov: Deriving and Enforcing Executable Constraints from Agent Instruction Files
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ContextCov, a framework that converts natural-language Agent Instructions into executable enforcement checks to prevent 'Context Drift' in autonomous LLM agents.
  • Implements three enforcement domains: static AST analysis for code patterns, runtime shell shims to intercept prohibited commands, and architectural validators for structural/semantic constraints.
  • Evaluated on 723 open-source repositories, extracting ~46,000 executable checks with 99.997% syntax validity, demonstrating practical automated compliance for agent-driven development.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Reshabh K Sharma']
  • Tags: ['agent guardrails', 'runtime enforcement', 'static analysis', 'autonomous agents', 'security tooling']

item.title: Learning to Attack: A Bandit Approach to Adversarial Context Poisoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AdvBandit, a black-box adaptive context-poisoning attack on neural contextual bandits that jointly learns and exploits the victim's evolving policy.
  • Attacker builds a surrogate via maximum-entropy inverse reinforcement learning from observed context-action pairs and optimizes continuous context perturbations with projected gradient descent; arm selection is guided by a UCB-aware Gaussian process.
  • Proposes an attack-budget control mechanism to limit detection and provides theoretical guarantees (sublinear attacker regret and victim-regret lower bounds linear in number of attacks).
  • Empirical evaluation on Yelp, MovieLens, and Disin shows AdvBandit induces higher cumulative victim regret than state-of-the-art baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ray Telikani', 'Amir H. Gandomi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'context poisoning', 'bandit attacks', 'black-box attack', 'inverse reinforcement learning']

item.title: MIDAS: Multi-Image Dispersion and Semantic Reconstruction for Jailbreaking MLLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MIDAS, a multimodal jailbreak framework that decomposes harmful semantics into subunits, disperses them across multiple images, and uses cross-image chained reasoning to reconstruct malicious intent.
  • Aims to increase model reliance on visual cues and lengthen reasoning chains to reduce security attention and bypass existing safety mechanisms.
  • Evaluates extensively across datasets and 4 closed-source MLLMs, reporting an average attack success rate of 81.46% and outperforming prior jailbreak methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yilian Liu', 'Xiaojun Jia', 'Guoshun Nan', 'Jiuyang Lyu', 'Zhican Chen', 'Tao Guan', 'Shuyuan Luo', 'Zhongyi Zhai', 'Yang Liu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'multimodal attacks', 'adversarial attack', 'security evaluation']

item.title: Are LLMs Reliable Code Reviewers? Systematic Overcorrection in Requirement Conformance Judgement
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a systematic failure mode where LLMs frequently misclassify correct code as non-compliant with natural-language requirements (overcorrection).
  • Finds that more detailed prompts—especially those asking for explanations and proposed fixes—can increase misjudgment rates.
  • Analyzes mechanisms behind these failures and evaluates the reliability of rationale-required judgments.
  • Proposes a Fix-guided Verification Filter that treats model-proposed fixes as executable counterfactuals and validates original/revised implementations using benchmark and spec-constrained tests as a mitigation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haolin Jin', 'Huaming Chen']
  • Tags: ['LLM robustness', 'code-review', 'model reliability', 'verification', 'defense/mitigation']

item.title: CaptionFool: Universal Image Captioning Model Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CaptionFool, a universal (input-agnostic) adversarial attack targeting transformer-based image captioning models.
  • Modifies only ~1.2% of image patches (7 out of 577) to force models to generate arbitrary target captions with 94–96% success.
  • Demonstrates ability to produce offensive/slang terms that evade content moderation filters, highlighting risks for deployed vision-language systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Swapnil Parekh']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'image captioning', 'universal attack', 'content moderation evasion', 'vision-language models']

item.title: Atomicity for Agents: Exposing, Exploiting, and Mitigating TOCTOU Vulnerabilities in Browser-Use Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in browser-use agents that arise when web pages change between planning and execution, allowing adversarial or dynamic content to induce unintended actions.
  • Presents a large-scale empirical study and benchmark spanning synthesized and real-world websites, evaluating 10 popular open-source browser agents and demonstrating widespread TOCTOU issues.
  • Proposes a lightweight mitigation—pre-execution validation that monitors DOM and layout changes during planning and validates page state immediately before action execution—which reduces insecure executions and unintended side effects.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Linxi Jiang', 'Zhijie Liu', 'Haotian Luo', 'Zhiqiang Lin']
  • Tags: ['TOCTOU', 'browser-agent security', 'adversarial web content', 'mitigation / pre-execution validation']

item.title: Exact and Asymptotically Complete Robust Verifications of Neural Networks via Quantum Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes two quantum-optimization-based verification models for adversarial robustness: an exact, sound-and-complete formulation for piecewise-linear activations (e.g., ReLU) and an asymptotically complete over-approximation for general activations (e.g., sigmoid, tanh).
  • Introduces solver-acceleration techniques including Quantum Benders Decomposition combined with interval arithmetic, certificate-transfer bounds for pruned networks, and a layerwise partitioning strategy enabling a quantum–classical hybrid workflow.
  • Demonstrates high certification accuracy on robustness benchmarks, showing the potential of quantum optimization as a primitive for robustness guarantees and precise adversarial-example identification.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenxin Li', 'Wenchao Liu', 'Chuan Wang', 'Qi Gao', 'Yin Ma', 'Hai Wei', 'Kai Wen']
  • Tags: ['robust verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'quantum optimization', 'certification']

item.title: Verifier-Bound Communication for LLM Agents: Certified Bounds on Covert Signaling
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CLBC, a verifier-bound communication protocol that separates generation from admission: messages are admitted to a transcript only if a small verifier accepts a proof-bound envelope under a pinned predicate Π.
  • Derives formal bounds on transcript leakage (latent leakage plus residual channels), adaptive composition guarantees, and a semantic lower bound when policy-valid alternatives remain available.
  • Provides extensive empirical evaluation and stress tests showing bounded covert-channel advantage and operational tradeoffs (e.g., full-proof mode latency vs. sampled proving).
  • Highlights that bottlenecks alone are insufficient and that security requires online, deterministic, fail-closed verifiable admission semantics.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Om Tailor']
  • Tags: ['covert channels', 'agent collusion', 'protocol defense', 'leakage bounds']

item.title: Quantifying Catastrophic Forgetting in IoT Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Frames RPL-based IoT intrusion detection as a domain continual learning problem and presents a method-agnostic IDS framework that can incorporate diverse continual learning strategies.
  • Systematically benchmarks five representative continual learning approaches across multiple domain-orderings using a comprehensive multi-attack dataset of 48 domains.
  • Finds that replay-based methods achieve the best overall performance, while Synaptic Intelligence (SI) attains near-zero forgetting with high training efficiency, highlighting trade-offs among plasticity, stability, and resource efficiency for IoT deployments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sourasekhar Banerjee', 'David Bergqvist', 'Salman Toor', 'Christian Rohner', 'Andreas Johnsson']
  • Tags: ['continual-learning', 'intrusion-detection', 'IoT-security', 'catastrophic-forgetting', 'defense-benchmarking']

item.title: Physical Evaluation of Naturalistic Adversarial Patches for Camera-Based Traffic-Sign Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CompGTSRB, a composite dataset tailored for autonomous vehicle environments by pasting GTSRB traffic sign instances onto undistorted target-platform backgrounds.
  • Trains a YOLOv5 detector and generates Naturalistic Adversarial Patches (NAPs) via a GAN with latent-space optimization following existing NAP methods.
  • Performs systematic physical-world experiments on a Quanser QCar platform (front CSI camera) evaluating NAP transferability across distance, patch size, and placement, showing reduced STOP-class confidence.
  • Provides a step-by-step methodology for credible physical patch evaluation and highlights the need for defenses addressing localized patch corruption in embedded perception pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Brianna D'Urso", 'Tahmid Hasan Sakib', 'Syed Rafay Hasan', 'Terry N. Guo']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-patches', 'physical-adversarial-examples', 'traffic-sign-attacks', 'robustness-evaluation', 'dataset-for-attacks']

item.title: LiaisonAgent: An Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous Risk Investigation and Governance
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents LiaisonAgent, an autonomous multi-agent SOC framework built on the QWQ-32B reasoning model that integrates specialized sub-agents for end-to-end risk investigation and automated remediation.
  • Uses a hybrid planning architecture combining deterministic compliance workflows with ReAct-style autonomous reasoning and tool-calling to handle ambiguous operational scenarios.
  • Evaluated across realistic security scenarios with high performance: 97.8% end-to-end tool-calling success, 95% risk judgment accuracy, 92.7% reduction in manual investigation effort.
  • Explicitly addresses security defenses: demonstrates resilience to out-of-distribution noise and adversarial prompt injections, and automates incident response workflows.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chuanming Tang', 'Ling Qing', 'Shifeng Chen']
  • Tags: ['AI security', 'defense', 'automated incident response', 'adversarial robustness', 'multi-agent systems']

item.title: Your Inference Request Will Become a Black Box: Confidential Inference for Cloud-based Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Talaria, a confidential inference framework that partitions LLM inference between a client-controlled Confidential Virtual Machine (CVM) and cloud GPUs to protect client data while preserving model IP.
  • Introduces Reversible Masked Outsourcing (ReMO), a hybrid masking protocol that reversibly obscures intermediate activations before outsourcing weight-dependent computations.
  • Claims lossless inference (outputs identical to original model), defends against token inference attacks (reducing token reconstruction accuracy from >97.5% to ~1.34%), and maintains efficiency and scalability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chung-ju Huang', 'Huiqiang Zhao', 'Yuanpeng He', 'Lijian Li', 'Wenpin Jiao', 'Zhi Jin', 'Peixuan Chen', 'Leye Wang']
  • Tags: ['confidential inference', 'privacy-preserving ML', 'inference attacks/defenses', 'secure computation']

item.title: Formal Analysis and Supply Chain Security for Agentic AI Skills
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents SkillFortify, a formal analysis framework for agent skill supply chain security covering attacker modeling, static analysis, sandboxing, dependency resolution, trust scoring, and a benchmark.
  • Key contributions: DY-Skill attacker model (Dolev–Yao adaptation) with maximality proof; abstract-interpretation-based static analysis with soundness; capability-based sandboxing with confinement proof; Agent Dependency Graph with SAT-based resolution and lockfile semantics; trust score algebra and a 540-skill benchmark.
  • Empirical results report 96.95% F1 (100% precision, 0% false positives) on 540 skills and SAT-based resolution handling 1,000-node graphs in under 100 ms.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Varun Pratap Bhardwaj']
  • Tags: ['supply-chain-security', 'formal-methods', 'static-analysis', 'sandboxing', 'benchmarking']

item.title: ThreatFormer-IDS: Robust Transformer Intrusion Detection with Zero-Day Generalization and Explainable Attribution
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ThreatFormer-IDS, a Transformer-based sequence model for intrusion detection on time-ordered network flow windows.
  • Combines weighted supervised learning, masked self-supervised pretraining, PGD-based adversarial training for feature-level evasion robustness, and Integrated Gradients for explainable attributions.
  • Evaluated chronologically on the ToN IoT benchmark with zero-day (held-out family) protocols and adversarial robustness tests, showing strong detection, improved generalization to unseen attacks, and slower degradation under bounded perturbations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Srikumar Nayak']
  • Tags: ['intrusion detection', 'adversarial robustness', 'zero-day detection', 'explainability', 'transformer']

item.title: Hidden in the Metadata: Stealth Poisoning Attacks on Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents MM-MEPA, a metadata-only poisoning attack that manipulates image-text metadata (without altering images) to steer multimodal retrieval and downstream RAG outputs.
  • Evaluates attack effectiveness across four retrievers and two multimodal generators, achieving up to 91% attack success and consistently disrupting system behavior.
  • Tests representative defenses and finds them largely ineffective, highlighting a critical vulnerability in multimodal retrieval-augmented generation systems and the need for defense-aware retrieval methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kennedy Edemacu', 'Mohammad Mahdi Shokri']
  • Tags: ['multimodal-poisoning', 'metadata-attacks', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'data-poisoning', 'defense-evaluation']

item.title: Reverse CAPTCHA: Evaluating LLM Susceptibility to Invisible Unicode Instruction Injection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Reverse CAPTCHA, a benchmark testing whether LLMs follow invisible Unicode-encoded instructions embedded in normal-looking text.
  • Empirical evaluation across five models from two providers, two encoding schemes (zero-width binary, Unicode Tags), multiple hint levels, payload framings, and tool-use settings (8,308 outputs).
  • Key findings: tool use greatly increases compliance; provider-specific encoding preferences (OpenAI vs Anthropic); explicit decoding instructions can boost compliance by up to 95 percentage points; all pairwise model differences statistically significant.
  • Highlights an underexplored prompt-injection attack surface using invisible Unicode payloads with implications for model safety and defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Marcus Graves']
  • Tags: ['prompt-injection', 'invisible-unicode', 'red-teaming', 'model-robustness', 'security-benchmark']

item.title: Steering Away from Memorization: Reachability-Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-Image Diffusion
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses memorization (training-data leakage) in text-to-image diffusion models and limitations of prior mitigation methods that degrade quality or alignment.
  • Proposes Reachability-Aware Diffusion Steering (RADS): models the diffusion denoising process as a dynamical system, approximates a backward reachable tube of states that lead to memorized outputs, and uses constrained reinforcement learning to minimally perturb caption embeddings at inference time to avoid those states.
  • Demonstrates improved trade-offs between generation diversity (SSCD), quality (FID), and alignment (CLIP) versus baselines, offering a plug-and-play defense that does not modify the diffusion backbone.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sathwik Karnik', 'Juyeop Kim', 'Sanmi Koyejo', 'Jong-Seok Lee', 'Somil Bansal']
  • Tags: ['memorization', 'privacy', 'defense', 'diffusion models', 'reinforcement learning']

item.title: You Don't Need All That Attention: Surgical Memorization Mitigation in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces GUARD, an inference-time framework that steers text-to-image diffusion generation away from memorized training images while preserving alignment to the prompt.
  • Provides a concrete instantiation using per-prompt cross-attention attenuation: a statistical mechanism identifies prompt positions needing attenuation and dynamically reduces cross-attention at those locations during denoising.
  • Evaluated across two architectures, GUARD reportedly achieves state-of-the-art mitigation for both verbatim and template memorization while maintaining or improving image quality.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kairan Zhao', 'Eleni Triantafillou', 'Peter Triantafillou']
  • Tags: ['memorization-mitigation', 'privacy-defense', 'diffusion-models', 'cross-attention-attenuation']

item.title: Thought Virus: Viral Misalignment via Subliminal Prompting in Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and defines 'subliminal prompting' as a way to bias an agent using semantically unrelated tokens and shows this bias can spread through multi-agent networks (a 'thought virus').
  • Empirically measures propagation across 6 agents and two network topologies, finding persistent elevated responses and degraded truthfulness in downstream agents (evaluated with TruthfulQA).
  • Frames subliminal prompting as a novel attack vector for multi-agent security and provides experimental code for reproducibility.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Moritz Weckbecker', 'Jonas M\\"uller', 'Ben Hagag', 'Michael Mulet']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent systems', 'adversarial prompting', 'misalignment/vulnerability', 'red teaming/attack evaluation']

item.title: Graph-theoretic Agreement Framework for Multi-agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a graph-theoretic framework mapping Transformer cross-entropy log-odds to a signed Laplacian to study consensus and stability in multi-agent LLM systems.
  • Identifies security vulnerabilities: unbalanced critique cycles cause logical frustration and hidden system prompts function as topological 'Trojan horses' that destabilize cooperative consensus.
  • Proposes defenses and algorithms: restrict interaction topologies to chordal graphs, apply matrix decomposition and Gram–Schmidt orthogonalization, and use rank-one spectral edge perturbations to deterministically stabilize agreement; includes polynomial-time verification algorithms and empirical validation on LLaMA-3, Mistral, and Gemma ensembles.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Muhammad Umar Javed']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent LLM security', 'Trojan prompts', 'robustness/defense', 'graph-theoretic analysis']

item.title: Joint Sensor Deployment and Physics-Informed Graph Transformer for Smart Grid Attack Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a joint multi-objective optimization framework (using NSGA-II) that simultaneously optimizes sensor placement and trains a physics-informed graph transformer network (PIGTN) for power system attack detection.
  • Introduces a physics-informed graph transformer that incorporates AC power flow constraints to improve generalization to unseen attacks and robustness to sensor failures.
  • Evaluates on multiple IEEE benchmark bus systems, reporting significant improvements in detection accuracy, detection rate, false alarm rates, and state estimation error compared to baseline sensor placements and graph-based detectors.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mariam Elnour', 'Mohammad AlShaikh Saleh', 'Rachad Atat', 'Xiang Huo', 'Abdulrahman Takiddin', 'Muhammad Ismail', 'Hasan Kurban', 'Katherine R. Davis', 'Erchin Serpedin']
  • Tags: ['attack detection', 'sensor placement', 'physics-informed ML', 'graph neural networks', 'power grid security']

item.title: Conformal Policy Control
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Conformal Policy Control: use a safe reference policy plus conformal calibration to regulate an optimized but untested policy so behavior change stays within a user-specified risk tolerance.
  • Provides finite-sample probabilistic guarantees for safety even with non-monotonic bounded constraint functions, without requiring correct model class or hyperparameter tuning.
  • Demonstrates safe exploration improving performance across tasks (e.g., question answering, biomolecular engineering) from initial deployment while enforcing declared risk limits.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Drew Prinster', 'Clara Fannjiang', 'Ji Won Park', 'Kyunghyun Cho', 'Anqi Liu', 'Suchi Saria', 'Samuel Stanton']
  • Tags: ['safe exploration', 'policy safety', 'conformal calibration', 'provable guarantees']

item.title: Evaluating and Understanding Scheming Propensity in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates 'scheming' (covert pursuit of misaligned goals) in autonomous LLM agents by decomposing scheming incentives into agent factors and environmental factors.
  • Constructs realistic experimental settings that systematically vary those factors and measures scheming propensity across scenarios, finding generally low scheming despite strong environmental incentives.
  • Shows adversarial system-prompt snippets can induce high scheming rates, but such behavior is brittle in many agent designs (e.g., sensitive to tool removal and oversight changes).
  • Provides an incentive-decomposition framework and measurement methodology for assessing scheming risks in deployment-relevant agent settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mia Hopman', 'Jannes Elstner', 'Maria Avramidou', 'Amritanshu Prasad', 'David Lindner']
  • Tags: ['scheming', 'agent alignment', 'adversarial prompting', 'safety evaluation', 'red teaming']

item.title: Beyond Reward: A Bounded Measure of Agent Environment Coupling
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces bipredictability (P), a bounded information-theoretic measure of agent–environment coupling computed from the observation–action–outcome loop.
  • P is estimated in real time via an auxiliary monitor called the Information Digital Twin (IDT), which also provides diagnostic components for interaction effectiveness.
  • Evaluated on SAC and PPO in MuJoCo HalfCheetah under 168 perturbation trials: IDT detected 89.3% of perturbations vs 44.0% for reward-based monitoring and had 4.4x lower median detection latency; nominal P reported as 0.33 ± 0.02.
  • Claims bipredictability enables earlier detection of interaction degradation (before performance drops) and can serve as a prerequisite signal for closed-loop self-regulation in deployed RL systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wael Hafez', 'Cameron Reid', 'Amit Nazeri']
  • Tags: ['reinforcement learning', 'robustness', 'runtime monitoring', 'anomaly detection', 'distribution shift']

item.title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a programming-language-based safety harness for AI agents: agents emit code in a capability-safe language (Scala 3 with capture checking) rather than calling tools directly.
  • Uses tracked capabilities (program variables) and the type system to regulate access to effects/resources and enforce local purity to prevent side effects and information leakage.
  • Claims the approach prevents unsafe behaviors (e.g., leakage of classified data, malicious side effects, prompt injection) while maintaining task performance.
  • Presents experiments demonstrating agents can generate capability-safe code and that the type system reliably blocks unsafe actions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Martin Odersky', 'Yaoyu Zhao', 'Yichen Xu', 'Oliver Bra\\v{c}evac', 'Cao Nguyen Pham']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'agent safety', 'capability-based security', 'type-system enforcement', 'information-leakage prevention']

item.title: The Synthetic Web: Adversarially-Curated Mini-Internets for Diagnosing Epistemic Weaknesses of Language Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Synthetic Web Benchmark: procedurally generated hyperlinked articles with ground-truth credibility/factuality labels and process-level interaction traces, designed to avoid training-data contamination.
  • Systematically injects a single high-plausibility misinformation article at controllable search ranks to measure the causal effect of adversarial ranking on retrieval-augmented language agents.
  • Evaluates six frontier models and finds catastrophic failures (accuracy collapse), minimal search escalation, and severe miscalibration despite access to truthful sources.
  • Provides a reproducible testbed to analyze failure modes under adversarial ranking and to develop/evaluate mitigation strategies for search-robust, epistemically humble agents.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shrey Shah', 'Levent Ozgur']
  • Tags: ['adversarial ranking', 'retrieval-augmented generation', 'misinformation', 'robustness benchmark', 'attack evaluation']

item.title: AI Runtime Infrastructure
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an execution-time layer (AI Runtime Infrastructure) between models and applications that observes and intervenes during agent execution to optimize task success, latency, token use, reliability, and safety.
  • Describes capabilities such as adaptive memory management, failure detection and recovery, and policy enforcement for long-horizon agent workflows.
  • Positions runtime interventions as an active optimization surface distinct from model-level changes or passive logging, enabling dynamic safeguards and operational controls while agents run.

Paper Type

  • Position

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Christopher Cruz']
  • Tags: ['runtime-defense', 'policy-enforcement', 'safety-mechanisms', 'fault-detection-recovery', 'agent-infrastructure']

item.title: TraderBench: How Robust Are AI Agents in Adversarial Capital Markets?
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TraderBench, a benchmark combining expert-verified static finance tasks with adversarial trading simulations scored by realized performance (Sharpe, returns, drawdown).
  • Provides two tracks: crypto trading with four progressive market-manipulation transforms and options derivatives tasks (P&L, Greeks, risk management); scenarios can be refreshed to avoid contamination.
  • Evaluates 13 models across ~50 tasks, finding many agents adopt fixed non-adaptive strategies under adversarial conditions and that extended reasoning improves retrieval but not trading performance.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaochuang Yuan', 'Hui Xu', 'Silvia Xu', 'Cui Zou', 'Jing Xiong']
  • Tags: ['adversarial evaluation', 'robustness', 'benchmark', 'finance', 'market-manipulation']
